Football.title: Chelsea team news ‘leaked’ AGAIN just days after club launched investigation following PSG clash
Football.content:
LIAM ROSENIOR has dropped Chelsea defender Wesley Fofana for Tuesday’s Champions League clash with PSG.

According to reports in France, the team has been leaked once more with Fofana BENCHED after his horror show in recent games.





Media in France also published the Chelsea XI prior to the first leg in Paris.

Asked about this after the game, manager Rosenior admitted he did not know it had gotten out.

He said: “I’m sure we’ll get to the bottom of that if that’s the case.

“These things now, they happen quite a lot. We get information about our opposition.



“I’m more worried about the end result than the beginning of the game.”

Chelsea are attempting to overturn a 5-2 deficit at Stamford Bridge after a late collapse at the Parc des Princes last Wednesday.

And Onze Mondial say that Fofana is among those to lose his place after struggles against PSG and then Newcastle.

Fofana has been criticised for his role in allowing Anthony Gordon an open goal in Saturday’s 1-0 defeat.



CASINO SPECIAL – BEST CASINO BONUSES FROM £10 DEPOSITS

The 25-year-old did not track Gordon’s run, allowing him an easy tap in.

He was also made a fool of by international team-mate Ousmane Dembele a few days earlier.

Dembele got away from Fofana to score after a blistering counter attacker from the UCL holders.

Although it is not said what other changes Rosenior might make, he could be forced into significant rotation.

With club captain Reece James also facing a spell on the side lines with injury, it remains to be seen if backup Malo Gusto is fit to play.



Jorrel Hato will replace Fofana next to Trevoh Chalobah, it is claimed.

Both James and Fofana have been carefully managed by Chelsea over the past 18 months due to their injury issues.

Fofana was named to start in each of the last three games having been rested for the FA Cup win over Wrexham, missing the loss to Arsenal a week earlier with suspension.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

As for James, he has completed each of the past three games, extending that to four of five, only missing the Wrexham clash as well.

He has picked up a hamstring strain, something that has kept him out for large periods since 2022.

Football.title: I won the European Cup before being forced to sign on at the dole office… now I’m a painter
Football.content:
COLIN BARRETT won the European cup as part of one of the most iconic English sides of all time.

However after retiring from football he found a new career, in painting and decorating.





After starting his career at Manchester City in 1970, Barrett was signed by Nottingham Forest in 1976, who were managed the legendary Brian Clough.

Over the next few years he helped Forest win promotion from the second division in 1977, and helped them win the first division, the League Cup and the Charity Shield in 1978.

In 1979, Forest were drawn against reigning European champions Liverpool in the first knockout round.

Barrett scored a stunning volley in a 2-0 win at the City Ground, sealing a vital first leg advantage.



On the goal, Barrett told The Telegraph in November 2025: “I’ve allegedly won the most important goal ever scored at the City Ground. There is supposedly a trophy for it but I’ve never seen it. We had it on video but my missus recorded Dallas over it. Can you believe that?“

Sadly a knee ligament injury 10 days after the game meant that he only made 12 professional appearances for the rest of his career.

This included the quarter final against Grasshopper and the semi final against Koln, as well as an appearance in the 1979 league cup final win against Southampton at Wembley.

Barrett missed the European cup final due to his injury and never played competitively for Nottingham Forest again, leaving for Swindon in 1980.



CASINO SPECIAL – BEST CASINO BONUSES FROM £10 DEPOSITS

Sadly he only made five appearances for the club and ended his professional career in 1981.

He told the Daily Mail in 2018 how he ended up as a painter and decorator after becoming unemployed: “I went down to the dole office and signed on. “What sort of job are you looking for?” they asked. “Well I have been a professional footballer”.

“I’m sorry, we don’t get many of those jobs in here”. I’ve got no qualms with it.”



After working in hospitality at his former club Forest, as well as jobs in a pub and building snooker tables, the former defender got into painting and decorating after people requested his services whilst he was painting his own house.

“I need mine painting”. Then another. So I carried on and became a painter-decorator,” Barrett said.

Barrett is now retired, however his name is always etched in Nottingham Forest history due to his viral contribution to their iconic first European cup win.

Football.title: David Raya levels above Dream Team rivals after 20th clean sheet of 2025/26
Football.content:
IT feels inevitable that David Raya (£4m) will finish this season as Dream Team’s top goalkeeper by a comfortable margin.

Arsenal’s No1 became the first player in his position to pass the 200-point milestone when he repelled Everton on Saturday.



Raya was required to make a couple of smart saves in what was a nervy game for the Gunners until late goals from Viktor Gyokeres (£4.7m) and Max Dowman (£3m) sent the Emirates into ecstasy.



The 2-0 win was the Spaniard’s 20th clean sheet in all competitions this campaign — no other keeper has more than 14 to their name at the time of writing.

MOST CLEAN SHEETS AMONG DREAM TEAM GOALKEEPERS THIS SEASON

20 – David Raya

14 – Gigi Donnarumma

13 – Dean Henderson

13 – Guglielmo Vicario

12 – Alisson

12 – Robert Sanchez

11 – Nick Pope

10 – Jordan Pickford

Raya has made the fewest saves (70) of all the keepers on the list, an indication of the protection provided by Gabriel (£4.9m), William Saliba (£4.3m), Jurrien Timber (£3.9m) and company.

And he’s yet to reach double figures for bonus points.

But clean sheets remain the most valuable metric for those who make a living between the sticks, hence why he’s top of the rankings with 206 points, 37 clear of his closest rival.

MOST POINTS AMONG DREAM TEAM GOALKEEPERS THIS SEASON

206 pts – David Raya

169 pts – Gigi Donnarumma

166 pts – Dean Henderson

164 pts – Robert Sanchez

163 pts – Guglielmo Vicario

151 pts – Nick Pope

146 pts – Alisson

146 pts – Robin Roefs

The battle to finish on the podium is a competitive one but it would take a monumental turn of form (or an injury) to prevent Raya claiming the gold medal.



This week’s European fixtures may strengthen his position further.

Mikel Arteta would have been disappointed with his side’s 1-1 draw away to Bayer Leverkusen last week but will feel confident of progressing to the quarter-finals when the Bundesliga outfit visit North London this evening.

There’s every chance Arsenal will be the only English team left in the Champions League come the quarter-final draw as Chelsea, Manchester City and Tottenham all suffered heavy defeats in the first leg of their last 16 ties.

Liverpool are also trailing on aggregate (0-1) ahead of Galatasaray’s visit to Anfield while Newcastle will have to resist Barcelona at the Camp Nou having drawn 1-1 at St James’ Park.

It may the case that Raya is one of very few keepers (or only) who will continue to score points in Europe from April.



In regards to upcoming Gameweek 29, it will be interesting to see whether Arteta sticks with Kepa Arrizabalaga (£2.8m) for the Carabao Cup final against Man City.

Raya has not played a single minute in either of the domestic cups this season but it’s often the case that managers revert to their first-choice XI when a trophy is at stake.

Pep Guardiola has a similar decision to make in regards to Donnarumma and James Trafford (£3.8m) as the latter started both legs of the semi-final against Newcastle.

Raya is by far and away the most popular goalkeeper in Dream Team with an ownership of 29% — no other asset in his position currently features in more than 10% of teams.

Football.title: Footballer suddenly dies just days after 22nd birthday as statement is issued – ‘profound sorrow’
Football.content:
TRAGEDY Footballer suddenly dies just days after 22nd birthday as statement is issued – ‘profound sorrow’

MEXICAN football is in mourning after Pablo Jurado Serafin died at the age of 22.

The shot-stopper’s sudden passing has sent shockwaves through Liga MX and former club Deportivo Toluca, where he came through the ranks after joining in 2019.





Serafin had been looking for a new club since being released in the summer of 2024.

And Deportivo Toluca have released a statement after the goalkeeper’s death was confirmed just days after celebrating his birthday.

A club spokesperson said: “Those of us at Deportivo Toluca Football Club deeply regret the passing of Pablo Jurado Serafin, a former goalkeeper of our institution.

“We send our condolences and best wishes to his friends and family.”



Serafin established himself as a key part of Deportivo Toluca’s youth set-up after featuring for the Under-17s, U20s and U23s.

He also enjoyed a brief spell on the books of Club Valle de Bravo before returning to his boyhood club to continue his development.

But Serafin was not handed a senior debut and was let go by the coaching staff when his youth career came to an end.

Mikel Arriola, president of the Mexican Football Federation, has paid tribute on social media.



In a statement posted on X, he said: “I am deeply saddened by the passing of Pablo Jurado Serafin.

“My condolences and solidarity go out to his family during this time of profound sorrow.

“I hope they find peace and strength in the face of this irreparable loss.”



Liga MX added: “We deeply regret the sensitive passing of Pablo Jurado Serafin.

“We join in the sorrow that overwhelms family and friends. May he rest in peace.”

The cause of Serafin’s untimely death has not been publicly disclosed.

Football.title: Thierry Henry shocked by Liam Rosenior’s Chelsea antics vs Newcastle as Carragher asks ‘how can you allow that?’
Football.content:
THIERRY HENRY slammed Chelsea boss Liam Rosenior for turning his back on play as a horror defensive error gifted Newcastle all three points.

The Arsenal legend, 48, was left shell-shocked after spotting the Blues chief looking away while the Magpies launched their match-winning counter-attack.





Tino Livramento sliced through the Chelsea press to find Joe Willock, who unselfishly teed up Anthony Gordon to grab the only goal of the showdown.

Pppearing as an analyst on Monday Night Football, Henry fumed: “I want to show you here [pointing at Rosenior turning his back on the play].

“I’ve been there. You’re disappointed that they allow that ball to go inside. But we’re not at the training ground here, where you can go, ‘let’s start again’.

“So since you talked about players do not react and they liked to be coached or coaches like to maybe not give them that freedom, then coach it.



“There is a mistake. We all know there is a mistake, so he’s not coaching it on the field.

“And, unfortunately, I said to you I’ve been there, he’s not even looking at what’s happening… goal.

“It’s easy to talk here. But that’s what they need to deal with and they didn’t.”

SUN VEGAS WELCOME OFFER: GET £50 BONUS WHEN YOU JOIN

Jamie Carragher was adamant that Chelsea trio Wesley Fofana, Trevoh Chalobah and skipper Reece James should take more responsibility.



They left a gaping hole in the middle of the park for Newcastle to exploit as the Magpies pushed for a vital win.

Carragher said: “In terms of Fofana, Chalobah and Reece James, they are all better players than me in almost everything.

“But this is why I played football. I would not have allowed that to happen because I would have made sure somebody reacted.



“Stop looking for your coach for instructions. You’re a coach on the pitch. That was my job as a player for Liverpool, to almost be a coach organising people around me.

“I was like that from a kid. Sometimes a coach has a plan, but the opposition do something different.

“What the coach can see from the side of the pitch is not what the players see on the pitch.”

Pointing to the giant space in the middle of Chelsea’s defence, allowing Livramento to find Willock with ease, Carragher added: “How can you allow that to happen?

“We’re talking about players playing for one of the biggest clubs in the world, costing huge fees.



RECOMMENDED STORIES

“Reece James has been described as the best player in his position in the world, and rightly so. He’s a fantastic player.

“But they allow that to happen because it wasn’t the plan before the game. The opposition have a plan as well.”

Football.title: Kyle Walker reveals dream transfer before retiring but admits ‘I don’t know if they will want me’
Football.content:
KYLE WALKER has revealed his “dream” to finish his career at boyhood club Sheffield United — but admits the move might never happen.

The Burnley defender recently called time on his England career after 14 years as an international footballer, sparking talk that he might be about to hang up his boots for good.





But Walker, 35, still has ambitions to keep on going in the professional game and isn’t thinking about finishing off just yet.

Speaking to Gary Neville on The Overlap, via Sky Bet, Walker said: “I’d like to [keep playing for another few years].

“It’s always been a dream of mine to play for Sheffield United again.

“To start there at six and finish 19, when I went to Tottenham. “I hope so [to go back again].

“I don’t know when that will be. I don’t know if they will want me.”

The six-time Premier League champion with Manchester City joined newly promoted Burnley last summer.

He has played 28 times for Scott Parker‘s side but looks set for relegation to the Championship.

BEST ONLINE CASINOS – TOP SITES IN THE UK

96-cap Walker spent the second half of last season on loan at Milan after falling out of favour with Pep Guardiola and some have claimed “his legs have gone.”



The 2023 Champions League winner, four-time Prem Team of the Season star, and 2012 Young Player of the Year is not done yet, though.

He continued: “I don’t know what it was like for you but once that time in my head comes where I’m done, I’d have to be true to myself.



“What does [Jamie] Carragher say? Leave the game before the game leaves you. That’s what’s in my head.

“I feel good. I’m 36 in May.

“It takes longer to recover, I can tell you that. One million per cent.”

Walker has made 438 top flight appearances and counting, racking up a haul of 14 major trophies along the way.

He is likely to go down with Burnley this season and will play at Sheffield United‘s Bramall Lane again next term if he stays with the Clarets.



Chris Wilder‘s team are mid-table in the Championship and not in the mix for promotion, surely too far off the bottom three as well.

And Walker isn’t giving up hope of becoming a Blade again.

He said: “And probably now I’m doing a lot more defending than I’ve ever done in my career, but I feel good.

“I think it’s more for me upstairs [mentally] and you fight your little battles in your head if you can keep doing it.

“If people look at you and say: ‘Oh flipping hell, what’s he doing still playing football.



“I don’t want to tarnish what I’ve done but I also have a level of respect for myself that I’ll do it on my terms.

“I’m not letting outside noise reflect what I want to do as a footballer because it’s a short career and people don’t understand that.

“It’s been inherited in us from the age of six when I started.

“I never played grassroots. I played for Sheffield United and that was it.

And all of a sudden, if you take that away from me, what am I going to do? What do I go into?



“I’ve not done anything else in my whole entire life except football, so it’s a big part of me and to let it go, it’s hard.

“But sometimes you have to let it go when you’re not good enough anymore.”

He will not be going to the World Cup over the summer having last played for England in June last year.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

Thomas Tuchel has since left him out of all of England‘s squads, turning back to Reece James and younger alternatives.

Walker’s time as a Three Lion is over but his arc is not complete yet.

Football.title: Iran demand Fifa move 2026 World Cup matches to another country as they slam Donald Trump over ‘safety’ fears
Football.content:
IRANIAN FA chiefs claim they are “in talks” with Fifa over switching their World Cup group games to Mexico.

The Iranians were drawn in Group G alongside Belgium, Egypt and New Zealand, with matches scheduled for Los Angeles and Seattle.





But with the US-led bombing and missile campaign, which killed former Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, still ongoing and now in its third week, the realistic likelihood of that is diminishing.

Last week, US President Donald Trump suggested Iran’s “life and safety” would be imperilled if they did play as scheduled, a move that hardened the country’s resolve to take part.

And Iran is now putting pressure on Fifa to switch their games and instead utilise the three Mexican stadiums in Mexico City, Monterrey and Guadalajara.

The Iranian state’s Mexico-based website declared: “Regarding the lack of cooperation from the US government in issuing visas and providing logistical support to the Iranian national football team in preparation for the 2026 World Cup, Abolfazl Psedniddeh (Iranian Ambassador to Mexico) suggested to Fifa that Iran’s matches in the tournament be moved from the United States to Mexico.”



That argument was confirmed as he added: “We reiterate that the United States is not cooperating with us on the issue of visas.

“We are interested in attending the World Cup, but the US government is not providing the necessary logistical or administrative support.

“Fifa can intervene so that the Iranian national team can participate in the World Cup, but in Mexico.

“The Iranian Ministry of Sports and Youth will make the final decision.



“We love the Mexican people very much, and for us, the best option is for our games to be held in Mexico.”

And the ambassador’s argument was echoed by Iranian FA boss Mehdi Taj, who said: “Since the President of the United States, Donald Trump, has explicitly stated that he cannot guarantee the safety of the Iranian national team, we certainly will not travel to the United States.

“We are currently negotiating with Fifa to have Iran’s World Cup matches held in Mexico.”

Fifa is understood to have received no official or formal approach asking for the games to be moved at this stage.

Click on a World Cup stadium to find out more

Doing so would be a logistical nightmare for the governing body as the 104-match schedule has been set for months and thousands of tickets have been sold for the three Iranian group games alone.



Only one match in the last-16 stage – England’s potential tie with the co-hosts in the Azteca – is scheduled to be played in Mexico, with no games after that.

And if Iran and the USA both finish second in their groups, they would meet in Dallas in the last 32.

As it stands, there are no signs that Fifa is prepared to make such a move although President Gianni Infantino does not want Iran to make history and become the first qualified team to miss a World Cup since France and India in 1950.

Football.title: Arsenal have best supersubs in Premier League and top table for goal contributions.. with bungling Man City worst
Football.content:
MAX DOWMAN stole the show when he came off the bench during Arsenal’s crucial win over Everton – and the Gunners are leading the way when it comes to subs making an impact.

No top-flight sides have had more goals and assists from those who started a showdown out of the starting line-up.





Dowman, 16, put in a dangerous delivery for Viktor Gyokeres’ opener and added his own name to the scoresheet as Arsenal grabbed a late victory against Everton.

And it took the Gunners’ tally of Premier League goal contributions from the bench up to 21.

The North Londoners have had players find the net 11 times after coming off the bench.

Statistics show another ten assists have been notched by Arsenal’s supersubs.



It will come as a huge boost for Mikel Arteta – who is looking to end a six-year wait to add major silverware to the trophy cabinet.

The Spaniard, 43, has also been calling his subs “finishers” to hammer home the important roll they are playing in the push for honours.

But it is a totally different story for Manchester City, who are Arsenal’s closest challengers for the Premier League title.

Pep Guardiola’s side fell NINE points off the pace thanks to their stalemate with struggling West Ham.



SUN VEGAS WELCOME OFFER: GET £50 BONUS WHEN YOU JOIN

City threw on Jeremy Doku, Rayan Cherki, Phil Foden and Tijjani Reijnders in a desperate attempt to narrow the gap.

But the quartet could not find the winner – and statistics highlight they have had little luck off the bench all season.

City have only had one goal and as many assists provided by a substitute in the Premier League this term.

On both occasions, they came from the boot of £34million summer signing Cherki.



It means that City have the worst record in the top-flight when it comes to goal contributions from the bench.

Everton are the only side to not have a single assist notched by a sub, but three goals have come from players thrown into the fray by chief David Moyes.

Despite sitting in the bottom half of the Premier League, Brighton boast the second-best record.

Fabian Hurzeler’s replacements have found the net nine times and grabbed five assists along the way.

Football.title: Baller League star slammed by own club for appearing in event 'without permission'
Football.content:
Baller League star slammed by own club for appearing in event 'without permission'

Wealdstone have released a statement after one of their players turned out for Prime FC in Baller League, prompting the National League club to open an investigation

View Image Sak Hassan pictured in Baller League last year (Image: Sky Sports)

National League side Wealdstone have opened an investigation into their own player after he appeared in Baller League without their permission. Stones winger Sak Hassan played for KSI's new team, Prime FC, in Monday's round of games.

‌



Hassan, 24, has been a regular for Wealdstone this season, scoring three goals and providing four assists in 32 appearances. The 13-time Somalia international last featured on March 7, helping the Stones beat Braintree Town 4-1 as they push to finish in the National League play-offs.

‌



But his most recent outing was in Baller League, a televised six-a-side tournament featuring influencers and ex-professionals. Hassan, who turned out for 26ers in season one of Baller Lague, scored the opener and laid on two assists as Prime FC won 7-3 against N5 FC at London's Copperbox Arena.

‌



The former Hashtag United star was on the scoresheet alongside ex-Manchester United prospect Ryan Tunnicliffe and Aston Villa striker Tammy Abraham's brother, Timmy. But Hassan, who left Hashtag to sign for Weadlstone last summer, now finds himself under investigation.

JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! Latest news, analysis and much more on Mirror Football's Facebook page

Baller League UK game abandoned after pitch invaded in violent scenes as pyrotechnics explode READ MORE :

KSI moment next to Andy Carroll in Dagenham stands says everything about new owner READ MORE :

In a statement posted on X, Wealdstone said: "We are aware that Sak Hassan, under contract with Wealdstone FC, appeared in this evening's Baller League. The club was not approached for permission for Sak to participate, nor were we made aware of his involvement in advance.

‌



"We will now be following up with both the Baller League and the player directly. No further comment will be made until those discussions have taken place."

The story goes deeper, too. KSI, real name Olajide Olayinka Williams Olatunji, recently became the owner of National League South outfit Dagenham and Redbridge, and he's been accused of tapping Hassan up.

Speaking to social media star Chunkz, KSI explained: "You mentioned him (Hassan) to me, so I talked to him and I was like, 'Bro, I've got a big thing coming'. At this point I didn't mention Daggers. I was like, 'Bro, I've got big things'."

‌



Content cannot be displayed without consent

Chunkz then interjected and asked: "Dagenham and Redbridge," to which KSI appeared to nod. The new part-owner continued: "So, there's that. And I also said, 'Bro, think of the club'."

KSI completed his investment in the Daggers earlier this month after the sixth-tier club were taken over by the Happy Fan Group, a consortium fronted by United States-based businessman John Grabowski. "I'm so excited to start this journey," KSI, who made his name on YouTube, said.

‌



"It's gonna be a rollercoaster for sure but I hope to bring Dagenham and Redbridge back to the glory days. And once we reach that point, I want to go even further. Reaching the Premier League would be a dream. And I believe it is 100 per cent doable.

"It will take a long time, so to the Daggers fans, please be patient. I want you to know that I am fully committed to making sure Dagenham and Redbridge is a team that everyone will know worldwide, and will be an exciting team to watch and support."

Football.title: Major stadium refuses to host World Cup matches despite splashing out £100m
Football.content:
Major stadium refuses to host World Cup matches despite splashing out £100m

The stadium was due to host matches at the 2030 World Cup, which is being held in Spain, Portugal and Morocco

View 2 Images Deportivo de la Coruna have pulled out of the chance of being a host venue for the 2030 World Cup. (Image: Jose Manuel Alvarez Rey/JAR Sport Images/NurPhoto via Getty Images)

The Spanish side Deportivo de la Coruña has pulled out of the chance to host games at the 2030 World Cup, despite having already heavily invested in its stadium. The next edition of the tournament following this summer’s in North America will be hosted by Spain, Portugal and Morocco.

‌



La Coruña's Riazor Stadium was initially selected as one of the host venues, but that selection was dependent on the stadium being increased from its current capacity of 32,490 to the 40,000 requirement outlined by FIFA. Initial plans were to increase the capacity of the stadium to 48,000.

‌



Work has already started to upgrade the stadium, with the club investing around £100 million. But it has now been confirmed that they are scaling back the restructuring and therefore withdrawing from hosting duties.

‌



Champions League co-efficient rankings latest as Man Utd and Liverpool sweat on extra spot READ MORE :

In a statement, club president Juan Carlos Escotet said: “We are making significant improvements to the stadium with a clear objective: to offer a better matchday experience to our fans, who truly deserve it.

“Deportivo’s ownership has already made an extraordinary effort with more than €100million invested in the last two years, primarily aimed at improving the club’s financial health and building a competitive squad.

JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! Latest news, analysis and much more on Mirror Football's Facebook page

‌



“Thinking about doubling its capacity not only lacks practical sense but would also lead to a significant drop in revenue due to excess capacity during the construction period.

“It would require an enormous level of investment given other social needs.

“We see no financial justification for doubling the stadium’s capacity when it is not necessary. It is a sports facility, and, therefore, it should be treated as such.”

‌



View 2 Images La Caoruna's Riazor Stadium. (Image: Angel Martinez/Getty Images)

La Coruña are currently in the second division of Spanish football. The team sit in second place in the table as they aim to make it back to La Liga, the league they won in the 1999/2000 season.

It’s still to be seen if a replacement stadium is lined up. In Spain, the Bernabeu, Camp Nou, Wanda Metropolitano and Athletic Club’s Anoeta Stadium have been selected.

Article continues below

In Portugal, games will take place at Benfica’s Estadio da Luz and Sporting CP’s Jose Alvalade. In Morocco, a new Hassan II stadium is being built with plans for it to have a capacity of 115,000.

Football.title: Scotland boss gives worrying take on his future before World Cup - 'They have to decide'
Football.content:
Scotland boss gives worrying take on his future before World Cup - 'They have to decide'

Steve Clarke will lead Scotland at their first World Cup since 1998 but is likely to go into this summer's tournament in North America with only a few months left on his contract

View 2 Images Steve Clarke is facing an uncertain future (Image: Robbie Jay Barratt - AMA/Getty Images)

Scotland manager Steve Clarke has revealed that he's yet to be offered a new contract, despite guiding them to their first World Cup in 28 years. Clarke's current deal with the Scottish FA will expire after this simmer's tournament, where his side have been drawn to face Haiti, Morocco and Brazil in Group C.

‌



The 62-year-old has guided Scotland to three major tournaments since taking charge in 2019. But his position came under scrutiny before the World Cup qualifying campaign, having finished bottom of their group at Euro 2024 and suffering relegation in the Nations League.

‌



Ahead of their friendlies against Japan and the Ivory Coast during the upcoming international window, the former Kilmarnock and West Brom boss says that the ball is in the SFA's court. Quizzed on his future, Clarke said: "Obviously the thing in my head is what do I do next, where do I go next, what's next for me?

‌



"I've qualified for three tournaments out of four. I think that's a pretty decent record." The Scot added: "Obviously, I'd like to do a little bit better at the tournament this summer, but that's something that we can address when we get out there.

JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! Latest news, analysis and much more on Mirror Football's Facebook page

Iran ask for drastic Mexico World Cup switch after USA decision amid Middle East war READ MORE :

‌



"Do the Scottish FA think that I'm the guy that can go forward and progress the squad into the next tournament and hopefully set us up for a further World Cup qualification in four years' time? That's what they have to decide.

"At the moment I don't really have a choice to make because they haven't put an offer in front of me and none of the club sides have put an offer in front of me either. I'm just sitting here talking to you guys, having a good time.

Should Steve Clarke be offered a new contract? Have your say in the comments section.

‌



View 2 Images Scotland will play Haiti, Morocco and Brazil in the group stage (Image: Stu Forster/Getty Images)

"I've just got to sit and see if any club approaches me between now and the end of the season, and then I'll have a decision to make."

Clarke went on to confirm that he's had a 'very brief conversation' with SFA chief executive Ian Maxwell regarding their future plans. As for the next step, the ex-Chelsea and St Mirren right-back explained: "I think I'm waiting on the Scottish FA now coming to me and saying, 'look, this is what we see as the future' and if I'm part of that then we can have that discussion."

Article continues below

Last month, Clarke was present at the draw for the 2026-27 Nations League. Having been relegated to League B, Scotland will face Switzerland, Slovenia and North Macedonia, but the question of who'll be in the dugout at Hampden Park when the competition begins in September remains unanswered.

Football.title: Man Utd chiefs 'reach transfer agreement' as Casemiro and Harry Maguire plan drawn up
Football.content:
Man Utd chiefs 'reach transfer agreement' as Casemiro and Harry Maguire plan drawn up

Manchester United have drawn up a contingency transfer plan should both Casemiro and Harry Maguire leave Old Trafford when their contracts expire this summer

View 3 Images Casemiro and Harry Maguire have been faultless since Michael Carrick has come in (Image: Getty Images)

Manchester United chiefs have agreed they have to 'sign two midfielders' in the wake of Casemiro's impending exit this summer and have drawn up a contingency plan should Harry Maguire also leave. United, who plan to strengthen again in the summer, have plans to make four major signings, it has been claimed.

‌



Casemiro, 34, announced in January that he will leave United when his contract expires at the end of the season, and his departure is set to leave the Reds with just two recognised first-team central midfielders in Manuel Ugarte and Kobbie Mainoo.

‌



And United are expected to act with 'club officials in agreement' that the Reds need to sign two players in that area. They have been monitoring Premier League midfielders with Newcastle United's Sandro Tonali among those shortlisted, according to The Sun.

‌



The Reds are also interested in Crystal Palace's Adam Wharton, Nottingham Forest's Elliot Anderson, Brighton's Carlos Baleba and Bournemouth's Alex Scott.

Man Utd chiefs issued clear transfer demand during crucial Aston Villa win READ MORE :

Wharton is keen to join a Champions League club and United are third in the table, six points clear of sixth-place Chelsea in the race for a spot in Europe's elite competition.

Manchester City, meanwhile, are the current frontrunners to sign Anderson, and it's thought that United might not be able to match the salary that their nearest neighbours are set to offer him.

‌



United wanted to sign Cameroon international Baleba last summer, but he has endured a disappointing season in a Brighton shirt and wasn't in the squad as they earned a 1-0 win at Sunderland on Saturday.

FOLLOW OUR MAN UTD FB PAGE! Latest United news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

Scott has been impressive for Bournemouth this season, and there has been talk that Tottenham and Manchester City are also keen on the 22-year-old England international.

‌



Casemiro could prove hard to replace with the Brazilian shining at the heart of United's midfield right now and scoring his seventh goal of the season in Sunday's 3-1 win against Aston Villa.

View 3 Images Casemiro is set to leave at the end of the season (Image: Getty Images)

United fans sang, 'One more year, one more year Casemiro, after the five-time Champions League winner opened the scoring for the Reds and he responded by pinching and holding the club's badge.

‌



But despite that plea from the Reds supporters, interim head coach Michael Carrick all-but dismissed the idea of Casemiro signing a new contract and remaining at United beyond the end of his current deal.

Carrick said: "The fact that it was decided makes things a little bit easier and everyone understands the situation, really. The impact he has had has been terrific, certainly, since I’ve been here and working with him. And he’s influence within the team and big moments and goals.

"Players come and go. Some are bigger, some are maybe more important than others at different times. But I don’t think it’s ever really about replacing like for like. I think you can go in different directions.

‌



"You understand what the balance of the squad will need, whether that’s on the pitch, off the pitch, leadership, positionally. There’s all sorts of different things going into it. Case has done some really, really good things, and certainly since I’ve been here, he’s been an absolute pleasure to work with."

View 3 Images Maguire's contract expires at the end of the season but there has been talk of an extension (Image: Robbie Jay Barratt - AMA, Getty Images)

As well as looking to recruit in midfield, United are exploring the centre-back market should the club fail to agree a contract extension for Harry Maguire. The 33-year-old England star is approaching the final three months of his deal but has been an ever-present since Carrick's appointment in mid-January - and the club are said to want him to stay.

‌



A renewal is being discussed, but Maguire will likely have to take a pay cut on his £180,000-a-week wages, while the 33-year-old is understood to want assurances over the length of the contract.

Lisandro Martinez and Matthijs de Ligt have been plagued with injuries across the season, leaving Maguire to come in and fill the void over the last nine matches following Carrick's arrival. Maguire has shone, with the ex-Leicester man poised to earn a recall to the England squad for the first time in 18 months this month.

United are also eyeing up a new left-sided winger in the summer transfer window. Patrick Dorgu had been thriving in a more advanced role up until suffering a long-term injury in the 3-2 win at Arsenal in January, while Matheus Cunha has started the last seven matches on the left-hand flank.

‌



Dorgu is considered a specialist left-back, but United might now view him as a winger, which could see them dip into the market for a left-sided defender instead, with Luke Shaw's contract up next summer.

A back-up goalkeeper is also on United's agenda, with Senne Lammens cementing his spot as the club's No.1, with Altay Bayindir acting as the No.2 in recent months, and Andre Onana spending the season on loan at Trabzonspor.

Football.title: Cristiano Ronaldo's family 'have no plans for Saudi return' after fleeing to Spain
Football.content:
Cristiano Ronaldo's family 'have no plans for Saudi return' after fleeing to Spain

Cristiano Ronaldo and his family left Saudi Arabia on his private jet when the war in the Middle-East began earlier this month

View 2 Images Cristiano Ronaldo's fiancé and children reportedly have no current plans to return to Saudi Arabia (Image: cristiano/INSTAGRAM)

Cristiano Ronaldo's family reportedly don’t know when they will return to Saudi Arabia after fleeing to Spain. The Al-Nassr star, his fiancé Georgina Rodriguez and their five children left Saudi, where the football legend has been playing since 2023, on March 3 aboard their private jet.

‌



They have since been staying in their Madrid mansion in the highly exclusive La Finca neighbourhood. The timing of the Portuguese icon’s departure from the Middle-East coincided with his children’s Spring school holidays, which were brought forward in light of the current war in the region.

‌



Spanish outlet Vanitatis reports that the decision was made by Ronaldo and Rodriguez to maintain a sense of normality for their children amid the escalating crisis.

‌



JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! All the best sports news and much more on our dedicated Facebook page

The report claims Ronaldo did not feel like his family were in danger but nonetheless felt unease and wanted to protect his children.

The publication went on to say sources close to Rodriguez, who hails from Madrid, claim she has no idea when she will return to Saudi, with the date of the family’s return to Riyadh not set and not dictated by the school calendar.

‌



Cristiano Ronaldo turns to unusual treatment in race to be fit for World Cup READ MORE :

Cristiano Ronaldo faces imminent financial blow that could scupper Man Utd takeover dream READ MORE :

However, there have been conflicting reports that Ronaldo leaving the Middle-East has less to do with the conflict which has engulfed the region this month and more to do with his ongoing recovery from a hamstring injury.

The 41-year-old suffered an injury during Al-Nassr’s match against Al-Fayha in late February. The official reasoning given for his trip to Spain is that he is prioritising his fitness and receiving specialised rehabilitation there with a personal physiotherapist.

View 2 Images Ronaldo is recovering from an injury in Madrid (Image: Anadolu via Getty Images)

‌



Al-Nassr’s Portuguese manager Jorge Jesus said: "Cristiano’s injury is more serious than we first thought. He will travel to Spain to receive treatment with a personal therapist, as many players do. We hope he returns soon and can help the team."

Ronaldo’s injury comes with the World Cup just around the corner. The 2026 World Cup this summer marks Ronaldo’s last opportunity to represent his nation at the iconic tournament and many expect that he will retire from his legendary career following its conclusion.

His rehabilitation in Madrid coincides with Rodriguez’s own Madrid Fashion Week commitments. While Ronaldo is expected to return to Al-Nassr training following his recovery, it is unclear whether Rodriguez and their children will return to Saudi following the end of Fashion Week.

Football.title: Bayer Leverkusen troll Arsenal's set-piece plan after playing them at their own game
Football.content:
Bayer Leverkusen troll Arsenal's set-piece plan after playing them at their own game

Bayer Leverkusen will resume their battle with Arsenal on Tuesday for the second leg of the teams' Champions League round-of-16 matchup, with the tie finely poised at 1-1

View Image Arsenal's tactics have caused controversy (Image: Alexander Hassenstein - UEFA/UEFA via Getty Images)

Arsenal have been trolled by Bayer Leverkusen for the second time ahead of their crunch Champions League clash. Leverkusen poked fun at the Gunners ahead of last week's first leg, saying 'no corners allowed' at their stadium.

‌



Ironically, it was Leverkusen who scored from a corner to take the lead against the set-piece specialists. Arsenal escaped from the Bay Arena with a 1-1 draw after Kai Havertz netted a late penalty against his former club.

‌



Now the teams will resume the tie in North London. On Monday, Leverkusen's account on X posted a photo of the Arsenal corner flag, but edited the image so that the pitch ended before it.

‌



"That's better," the Bundesliga club wrote as the caption. Users loved the joke, with one posting: "Best football admin ever." Another said: "Nah, admin doing the lord's work."

FOLLOW OUR ARSENAL FB PAGE! Latest Gunners news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

11 Arsenal stars who could be sold as Andrea Berta makes transfer priority clear READ MORE :

But some Arsenal fans didn't see the funny side. "Enjoy the engagement while it lasts," one wrote. A second added: "Didn't you score from a corner?"

‌



Leverkusen also shared another post of their 'Arsenal stadium checklist'. It read: "Bring tape to cover the power outlets. Paint the corners round. Avoid penalties (important!). Dark arts???"

As the kings of set pieces in the Premier League, Arsenal have 21 goals from dead-ball situations this season. But it was Leverkusen who used them best in Germany, as Robert Andrich broke the deadlock by heading home Alejandro Grimaldo's corner at the start of the second half.

It's not all fun and games, though. Leverkusen manager Kasper Hjulmand has questioned the legality of Arsenal's set-piece tactics, raising concerns over whether blocking should be allowed when the ball isn't close.

‌



Who'll reach the quarter-finals? Give us your prediction in the comments section.

Content cannot be displayed without consent

"It's a big challenge but I have to say, I'm a bit confused by these offensive blocks," Hjulmand told a pre-match press conference. "I don't know if it's allowed under the rules when the ball is very far away."

‌



The winner of Tuesday's second leg will progress to the quarter-finals and take on either Sporting Lisbon or Bodo/Glimt, who boast a 3-0 lead on aggregate. Asked what he expects of Leverkusen, Mikel Arteta replied: "Very much the same and watching them play against Bayern Munich at the weekend, very similar.

"So again, really well coached, a team with a very clear structure, really good individuals. Obviously we are in the stage, in the competition that we are in right now, so we know what we have to do, and in front of our crowd, with our people, putting all the energy in the world again, hopefully we are going to do it."

Football.title: Did 'feisty' Hamilton vs Leclerc battle ignite Ferrari rivalry?
Football.content:
Lewis Hamilton and Charles Leclerc delivered a thrilling battle for the podium at the Chinese Grand Prix, which the seven-time world champion called the "best racing I’ve ever experienced in Formula 1".

Hamilton and Leclerc demonstrated the best of F1's new regulations with several overtakes and re-overtakes around the Shanghai International Circuit on Sunday, having also got close in Saturday's Sprint too.

The pair remarkably only made minor contact once, or a "kiss" according to Hamilton, who came out on top to claim his first podium as a Ferrari driver.

"It felt like go-karting, back and forth, back and forth, and you could really position your car in a nice way where there was a thin piece of paper between us sometimes," said Hamilton.

"But we didn't exchange any paint. I think that's down to great drivers and respect."

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Lewis Hamilton and Charles Leclerc go head-to-head in their Ferraris at the Chinese GP

George Russell had a front row seat for much of the battle, before finding a way past the Ferrari duo, and was amazed by how hard the racing was.

"I was just waiting for the two of them to collide and somehow they didn't. It was some of the most aggressive racing I've seen for a while," he said.

"If I wasn't trying to win the race, I'd have been enjoying the battle, but obviously watching Kimi [Antonelli] pull away during those points it was a little bit annoying."

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Kimi Antonelli, George Russell and Lewis Hamilton watch back the key moments from the Chinese GP in the cool-down room

Villeneuve questions if Ferrari fighting will cost them vs Mercedes

It was Leclerc who started the on-track battling on Sunday just before half distance of the 56-lap Chinese Grand Prix with both Ferraris in podium positions and Russell in fourth.

Although the Ferrari fight was exciting, the pair were not driving in an optimal way and Russell was able to get by when he had appeared stuck.

Sky Sports F1's Jacques Villeneuve thinks Hamilton and Leclerc both want to be "the man" at Ferrari, even if it means losing time to their rivals by battling.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Charles Leclerc complained about Lewis Hamilton over the team radio as Ferrari's nearly touch in Sprint at the Chinese GP

"Lewis is feisty and fighting. He was quite cool, minding his own business, not letting Antonelli get away too far," said the 1997 F1 world champion

"But then Leclerc decided not to play the team game and played the Leclerc game, caught him by surprise, but he should have seen it coming. The same thing happened in the Sprint.

"It turned out OK but it was a little bit extreme and destroyed Ferrari's race. They were running second and third, fighting so hard, destroying their tyres.

"At some point, if you keep taking lap time away from each other, it allows Mercedes to get away and they are not that far from Mercedes with a car that can race. Mercedes should worry a little bit about Ferrari coming back because they are not that far behind in points."

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Charles Leclerc says Ferrari didn’t fully optimise their race but it didn’t affect the final result, as he reflects on an entertaining battle with team-mate Lewis Hamilton

Ferrari team principal Frederic Vasseur says he "trusts" his drivers and it would have been "unfair" to call the battle off.

"I have a huge respect for both of them. They are professional and I think it makes sense in this situation to let them race," he said.

"I know perfectly that it can also look completely stupid half an hour later, but at the end of the day I think it's also the best way to build up a team. We need to have this kind of emulation into the team to improve.

"As long as it's done like it was done in China, even a couple of times in the radio they told us that they had good fun, I don't want to freeze the position."

Image: F1 Drivers' Championship after Chinese GP

Will Ferrari have problem of two evenly matches drivers?

Last year, Leclerc was clearly quicker than Hamilton for much of the season so Ferrari rarely had the problem of their drivers scrapping for the same piece of track.

The opening two races this year have shown Leclerc and Hamilton are much more evenly matched, so the team may need to step in if the drivers go over the limit, or face the awkwardness of a collision - something F1 history shows is an inevitability when team-mates are regularly fighting.

There was perhaps more eagerness in China between the Ferrari pair to be ahead of one another knowing there was a high chance of a Safety Car.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player George Russell goes backwards as both Lewis Hamilton and Charles Leclerc overtake the Mercedes driver at the Chinese GP after a Safety Car restart

Earlier in the race, Leclerc had to stack behind Hamilton in the pit stops when most drivers pitted after Lance Stroll caused a Safety Car when he came to a halt.

The increased unreliability of the 2026 cars means drivers may suddenly pull over to the side of the track, therefore increasing the possibility of a Safety Car or Virtual Safety Car, where drivers may get a cheaper pit stop.

At the Miami Grand Prix, on one of the few occasions last year when the Ferraris were battling, the team were slow to deploy team orders when Hamilton appeared faster and was stuck behind Leclerc.

The British driver stated "have a tea break while you're at it" on the team radio during one of the heated exchanges with his engineer Riccardo Adami.

But, Hamilton has appeared to be much happier with his new race engineer Carlo Santi, who has been appointed on an interim basis.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player New footage of a wholesome exchange between Lewis Hamilton and Charles Leclerc after team orders dispute at the Miami Grand Prix

"I think I came into the season with the confidence that I used to have, and I think I've stuck at it," said Hamilton.

"It's the hardest thing just to get a podium compared to a lot through my career and it was the biggest challenge to get that, and I just had to bide my time. I think coming into the season I really cultivated that really positive mental attitude and I'm taking that forward with me."

If Hamilton continues his strong start to 2026, he will be confident of a spectacular first victory in red. However, a fast Hamilton will give Ferrari a headache, albeit a nice one, as their two drivers will surely end up battling more - but it cannot be to the detriment of losing out to Mercedes.

Formula 1 next heads to the iconic Suzuka Circuit for the Japanese Grand Prix on March 27-29 live on Sky Sports F1. Stream Sky Sports with NOW - no contract, cancel anytime

Football.title: Today on Sky Sports Racing: Sintra seeks first win for Juddmonte at Wolverhampton
Football.content:
It's all about Wolverhampton today, with all nine races LIVE on Sky Sports Racing from 4.28pm.

4.28 Wolverhampton - Juddmonte's Sintra seeks first win

Andrew Balding's Sintra will face six rivals in the opening Daily Profit Boosts At betmgm.co.uk Novice Stakes over the extended mile. The son of Kingman is a half-brother to Listed winner Windlord and comes here seeking a first success after finishing third on debut last month. The jockey booking of Cheltenham Festival-winning jockey Colin Keane is a notable one, with the six-time Irish champion coming here for just his fourth ever ride at Dunstall Park.

Richard Hughes' three-year-old Nobody Knows was a €320,000 Breeze-up purchase and justified favouritism on debut at Ayr in September, scoring by two lengths. The son of Wootton Bassett has entries in both the Irish 2,000 Guineas and Irish Derby so may be one to keep an eye on as the season progresses.

Derby-winning jockey Adam Kirby saddles his third ever runner as a trainer here with My Mate Mackley, who finished last of eight on his sole two-year-old appearance at the back end of last year. He will be looking to provide Kirby with his second winner since his transition to training.

Of the newcomers, Mick The Hat could be the most interesting for Ralph Beckett, given he was purchased for 105,000gns as a yearling.

6.00 Wolverhampton - Bomb Squad and Ramon Di Loria lock horns again

A field of eleven will contest the Always Gamble Responsibly At BetMGM Handicap over seven furlongs at Wolverhampton on Tuesday and the consistent Bomb Squad will be looking for a third win from his last four starts here for John Mackie. If the eight-year-old is to land successive course and distance wins then he will have to defy a 4lb penalty for his win four days ago.

Ramon Di Loria was only half a length behind Bomb Squad four days ago and is 4lb better off today due to Bomb Squad's penalty. Ewan Whillans will be hoping for another bold showing from his gelding, who has finished in the top three in six of his last seven runs.

John O'Shea's Punchbowl Flyer has been running to a fair level of form of late without getting his head in front, notably when finishing a runner-up to winning machine Beauzon two starts back. He is coming here looking for a first win since August.

Course and distance winners Landlordtothestars and Von Krolock are others to note, whilst Amelia's Joy is an intriguing contender in first-time cheekpieces for Michael Herrington.

7.30 Wolverhampton - In-form Alasrae bids to complete the hat-trick

A field of ten have assembled for the Bet £10 Get £40 With BetMGM Handicap over the minimum trip, with Alasrae on the hat-trick hunt for Billy Loughnane and George Boughey. After failing to win in seven starts as a two-year-old, the son of Havana Grey went down by just a neck on his first run of the year and improved on that performance to record a first win next time out. He followed that up with a nose success at Kempton 13 days ago and drops back to the five-furlong trip here in pursuit of the hat-trick.

The £350,000 breeze-up purchase Fortification is an intriguing runner for Archie Watson, who will be hoping first-time blinkers will help bring out enough improvement in his three-year-old to record a second career success.

The James Tate-trained pair of Daneh Of Dandy and Dark Angel Star are others to note with the former having her first start on the all-weather, while course and distance winner Henrythenate and Brian Ellison's Temple Of Athena are others to keep an eye on in this field of ten.

Football.title: Henry defends Arteta but admits neutrals 'don't want Arsenal to win' title
Football.content:
Thierry Henry has said a lot of neutrals want “anyone but Arsenal” to win the Premier League as he defended his former side’s style of play under Mikel Arteta on Monday Night Football.

The former Arsenal captain, top scorer in the Invincibles side which last lifted the Premier League trophy in 2003/04, said Arteta had been "asked to find a way, and he has" with the Gunners now nine points clear at the top of the table, having played a game more than second-placed Man City.

Arsenal's style has been criticised largely owing to their reliance on set-pieces - which have contributed to more than a third of their goals this season - despite a transfer spend of more than £250m this season, primarily on attacking talent.

Henry appeared puzzle surprised by the lack of support for Arsenal from neutrals in their quest to end a 22-year wait for the title up against Pep Guardiola's City, winners in six of the last eight seasons.

"A lot of people are like 'anyone but Arsenal to win the league'," Henry said on Sky Sports. "I wanted Liverpool to win the league when I was playing because I wanted Jamie [Carragher] and Stevie G to win the league.

"Whether I like Liverpool or not, you root normally for the underdog, for the team that could not do it or failed. But for some reason, a lot of people don't want Arsenal to win the league."

Sky Sports pundit Jamie Carragher added: "It is very strange. Liverpool and Man Utd fans, even though City are the local rivals for United and Liverpool have been fighting City for titles, they are almost looking and thinking I'd rather City win it.

"There is something about Arsenal and the way they have gone about it, whether you like it or not.

"Whether it is the style of football, Arteta on the side of the pitch, what Fabian Hurzeler was saying. Maybe it is the fanbase on social media, which gets a bit of criticism, that maybe rubs people up the wrong way."

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player FREE TO WATCH: Highlights from Arsenal’s match against Everton in the Premier League

'Nonsense' - Carra and Henry respond to Arsenal criticism

In his defence of Arteta's approach, Henry stopped short of saying he was a fan of the football Arsenal play. He says his feelings on the matter are irrelevant as the Gunners seek to end their title drought.

"I don't have to like it as an Arsenal fan, but I surely respect it," Henry said. "After 22 years, I'm not saying that we're going to win the league but no matter how they're going to do it, I'm going to respect it.

"It doesn't matter what I like, it doesn't matter what my mum likes or my dad likes. We asked Mikel Arteta to find a way, he did. It's as simple as that. I want to win the league, it's been 22 years.

"For a very long time, Arsenal have been accused of being boys. Not being able to keep a lead, getting bullied. Can they win ugly? That's exactly what the team is doing, and mastering that.

"Along the way, have they lost what they were about when Mikel Arteta arrived? Sometimes in the game when you try to do something to evolve from Pep, evolve from Slot, you can forget what you were a bit. Three years ago people were talking about what good football Arsenal played, but with those accusations I mentioned.

"Now that they are doing it, people are not happy about it."

Carragher added: "Some of the nonsense that has been spoken about Arsenal from other people in the game about an asterisk being by their name if they win the league or 'I won't recognise them if they win the league' is absolute nonsense.

"There is no right or way to play football. It is about being the best at what you do. Right now, whether you like it or not as an Arsenal supporter, they are going to be remembered as the set piece team."

This season, Arsenal have the tightest defence of any period during Arteta's reign but are scoring at a lower rate than during 2022/23 and 2023/24, when they were more widely praised for their style of football - and were less reliant on set-piece goals.

Henry questioned whether the Gunners were still too closely associated to the Arsene Wenger era almost a decade on from his retirement, given they had twice won the First Division under predecessor George Graham while being dogged by accusations of dull football.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Thierry Henry and Jamie Carragher reflect on Max Dowman's magical moment for Arsenal

He said: "What is the Arsenal way? My way? George Graham? What is it? If you think about how the '1-0 to the Arsenal' came about, that's closer to Graham than what we were playing, but how Arsenal were playing was closer to us and what Arsene was trying to do. But Arsenal won under Graham and Wenger. What's the issue here?"

Henry pointed to his own experience of coming up against Jose Mourinho's Chelsea, who were also criticised for their pragmatic style when lifting their first Premier League trophy, 12 points ahead of the Gunners, in 20024/05.

Arsenal scored 15 goals more than the Blues in that season but conceded more than double Mourinho's side, who ended Man Utd and Arsenal's title monopoly which had stretched back to 1996.

"Frank Lampard finished that season as their top goalscorer with 13 goals," Henry said. "They were very difficult to beat, outstanding on the break. Everyone praised Mourinho and Chelsea, and rightly so. They found a way to stop the duo.

"So well done. Whether you like Chelsea or not, well done."

Carra: Arteta 'almost different to any manager we've seen'

Jamie Carragher was similarly unbothered by Arsenal's style and instead singled out Arteta for his flexibility in pivoting from following former boss Pep Guardiola's style of play to what he has termed a more "Mourinho-esque" approach - a sea change in philosophy he feels has never been seen by a Premier League title winner before.

"It's almost different to any manager we've seen, certainly in terms of the Premier League," he told Monday Night Football. "What you've got when he comes in in 2019 is that it's mid-way through a season, Arsenal win the FA Cup and get some big scalps, it's not a great team but they finish eighth.

"A couple of years later they finish fifth and you feel the fans are really behind him. I remember the last game of the season where there was this feel-good factor that they were going somewhere.

"There's then the two seconds after that, and that's where I think there is the shift. When Arteta first came in, we put him in a box. We said he's a Pep Guardiola coach and he'll play that football at Arsenal. With other managers, you know what Klopp is from day one. We knew what Pep was from day one.

"The season where they finished fifth and then second, the football was fantastic. But from last season, he's morphed from one style into almost a Mourinho-style. I've been calling Arsenal and Arteta that for a couple of years, but not as a criticism.

"Mourinho and Guardiola are two of the most successful managers of the last 20 years. They have different ways of going about it, but the fascinating thing about Arteta is he's started with one and morphed into the other. You don't normally see that.

"That first season they finished second, they went to Man City and got beaten up and it cost them the league. Ever since then, every signing they've made has been a physical profile. That wasn't the case when he first came to the club. It's about power and strength."

Football.title: Is title-race pressure on Hearts as Celtic, Rangers close the gap?
Football.content:
Three teams. Three points between them. Eight games to go. One unmissable title race.

The battle for Scottish Premiership glory served up another twist in the tale as Hearts' five-point advantage was cut to just two when champions Celtic beat Motherwell, before the league leaders lost at Kilmarnock on Saturday.

Meanwhile, Rangers' win at St Mirren the following day saw them stay a point behind their Old Firm rivals and move to within striking distance of the Jambos.

The only thing predictable about this title race is... its unpredictability! Well, here's what those brave enough to guess what will happen have to say, plus your chance to vote for who finishes top of the table and more...

What Opta predict and the bookies think...

There are just three games to go until the Premiership split, when the table breaks into a top six and bottom six. That will set up five massive matches to determine the destination of this season's title.

Yes, Hearts lead the way, and their fearless challenge has created a run-in rich with jeopardy and showered in excitement.

However, either half of the Old Firm have got their hands on the trophy every season for the last 41 years and are now closing in on the top.

Monterosa Monterosa , which may be using cookies and other technologies. To show you this content, we need your permission to use cookies. Celtic have won 13 of the last 14 top-flight titles alone, and perhaps it is for that reason they are now the bookies' favourites, despite being unable to beat Hearts or Rangers in the league this season.

Well, Hearts are second on that list of favourites, and perhaps they can take some comfort from Opta's super-computer.
