Angel Gomes was Wolves’ half-time introduction to disrupt Brentford’s plan of hitting their front players as soon as possible, bypassing André and João Gomes, neither of whom will be flying back with Thiago for Brazil duty but who soon began to exert heavy midfield influence.

Wolves’ renewed confidence was evidenced by Jackson Tchatchoua’s volleyed snapshot. Next, Armstrong hit the foot of a post with Kelleher stranded. With Wolves showing that even the Premier League’s bottom club is laden with talent, the contest had tilted.

“We owe it to the football club, ourselves, the fans to keep fighting all the way until the 38th game, so we’ll do that,” said Edwards, whose team must now wait 25 days to face West Ham.

A panicked clearance from Nathan Collins personified a growing anxiety among Brentford fans, players and their manager. “We didn’t do the basics as well as we did in the first half and, ultimately, it cost us,” said Andrews.

Angel Gomes zipped a shot wide before eventually Wolves’ growing pressure told. João Gomes’ ball across the Brentford box beat everyone except Arokodare, who just three minutes after coming on, stooped to head home. His later header against the bar came from Hugo Bueno’s cross, disappointment for Wolves, denied that first away win even if their comeback had inflicted heavy damage on Brentford.

The Brentford substitute Reiss Nelson’s header wide was a chance to steal victory passed up by a poor execution that had become endemic within his teammates since the break. It would also have been cruel on Wolves, a team surely doomed yet still refusing to lie down.

Football.title: Cesc Fabregas tipped to become Arsenal manager as he draws comparisons to ‘second father’
Football.content:
Cesc Fabregas tipped to become Arsenal manager as he draws comparisons to ‘second father’

Como president Mirwan Suwarso has claimed Cesc Fabregas is capable of managing Arsenal one day - but he must first find his successor.

Former Gunners midfielder Fabregas is thriving on the touchline in Serie A, with his side fourth in the table after a 2-1 win over Roma.

6 Como's president has admitted Fabregas could 'go to Arsenal, Barcelona or Chelsea' Credit: Getty

The 38-year-old is currently in his first job as a senior manager, having taken over Como on an interim basis in November 2023.



Fabregas helped mastermind the club's return to the top flight of Italian football for the first time in 21 years as an assistant while obtaining the necessary UEFA coaching badges.

The former Arsenal, Barcelona and Chelsea midfielder – also a part-owner at Como – then took the manager job permanently in 2024.

I Lariani finished tenth in their first season back in Serie A, and have since gone from strength to strength in Fabregas' second full season.

Como currently occupies a Champions League spot after 29 matches, having done the double over the 36-time champions this campaign.



Fabregas told he’s good enough to manage Arsenal

Club president Suwarso now admitted to Revista Undici that his manager's talents could see him end up at a former club one day.

“Fabregas is really very important for us, but we would be stupid not to think that one day he could go to Arsenal, Barcelona or Chelsea,” he said.



“If he continues to win with us or in any case to be successful, even if I cannot speak for him, I believe he could have bigger dreams.

“Having said that, I hope that as long as he is here he will help us create a success plan also for those who will have to replace him.



6 Fabregas's Como have lost just five of their 29 Serie A matches this season Credit: Getty

“For me, when he leaves, if he ever leaves, he should be the one to choose who will take his place. He should be involved in the choice.

“Being a member of the Board of Football, he will have to help us appoint the next coach.”

Fabregas' impressive stint at Como hasn't gone unnoticed around Europe, having been heavily linked to Bayer Leverkusen last summer.

He was also on Leicester’s shortlist following the Foxes' relegation last April, with Inter Milan among his more recent suitors as well.



European football expert Andy Brassell told talkSPORT.com: "When I think of what Cesc Fabregas is doing at Como, you can't help think of Arsene Wenger, really, because he's got a very particular role in the club and a very particular role in the way that the club and the team are developing and establishing itself in Serie A.

"Cesc is someone who clearly has a very deep understanding of the game, clearly connects very well with all his players."

6 Cesc Fabregas has previously said Arsene Wenger was ‘a father figure’ for him at Arsenal Credit: Getty Images - Getty

Arsene Wenger comparison

"What they've done so sensibly is getting a load of young players that he can shape and that he can get through to," Brassell added.

"I think in terms of their energy, their versatility, their ability to grow and their ability to get better players than they would do otherwise, given their historical size or lack of it, it's a really good policy.



"Fabregas has got a part in that, and he's got, when I was making the Arsene Wenger comparison, not like for like in every sense, but in the sense that he's fully invested and financially invested in the club as a part shareholder as well.

"It makes him closer to an old-fashioned manager than just a head coach, a little bit like Arsene Wenger, as we were talking about before.

"You go back to last summer, and Leverkusen wanted him, Leipzig wanted him, Inter wanted him.

6 Como have the second-best defensive record in Serie A and the second-best goal difference in Italy Credit: Getty

6 However, his team also boast the best possession stats to show his style of play Credit: Getty



"If you take away what we know about Como and their current financial situation, I'd have said to you three years ago that the manager of Como, let's say the unnamed manager of Como, is of interest to Leverkusen, Leipzig and Inter. You're thinking, 'Well, he's off to one of them, isn't he?'

"They're able to build and hold on to because he's fully invested in the project, just like Wenger was at Arsenal. It's really interesting.

"They've beaten Juventus twice. They've gone and got themselves a draw at Milan in the last week or so as well.

"So, they're brave enough to play their football and try and dominate the game against big, lauded, historically strong opposition as well, which I think says a lot about Fabregas and his personality."

6 Fabregas is an Arsenal legend, but broke fans' hearts when he returned to Barcelona in 2011 Credit: Getty



Comparisons to Wenger will come as little surprise to Fabregas, who left Barcelona's La Masia academy to join the Frenchman at Arsenal.

After joining the Gunners in September 2003 as a 16-year-old, Fabregas made his debut just a month later.

Over an eight-year spell, the Spaniard made 303 appearances for Arsenal, scoring 57 goals and registering 95 assists.

The ex-Gunners captain helped Arsenal to FA Cup glory, and was part of the side that reached the Champions League final in 2006.

"Arsene Wenger is like a second father to me," Fabregas told DAZN Italia.



"Obviously, he worked a lot on transitions, attacking the space, something that is even more important in modern football.

"I learned a lot from him and want my team to do that."

Football.title: Ashley Cole questioned for taking surprise first job in Italian second tier despite ‘earning his stripes’ in England
Football.content:
Ashley Cole questioned for taking surprise first job in Italian second tier despite ‘earning his stripes’ in England

Darren Bent was caught off-guard by Ashley Cole's first managerial role and claimed he'd have been better off at a Championship side.

Cole was appointed manager of Serie B side Cesena until the end of the season on Sunday, although he could secure a prolonged stay in northern Italy should certain criteria be met.

4 Cole has done it all as a player, but can he enjoy success as a manager? Credit: Getty

It marked the 45-year-old's first senior managerial role of his coaching career having cut his teeth as an assistant at Chelsea, Everton, Derby County, Birmingham City and England.



Speaking on talkSPORT Drive, Bent pointed out Cole has 'got the credentials' having learned his trade in England and working with some impressive players.

It's why the Chelsea great's decision to take the managerial plunge in the Italian second tier was all the more perplexing.

Cole 'earned his stripes', but Cesena move still surprising

"It does surprise me that he's gone to a second division team in Italy," Bent said.

"I thought he's got the credentials, certainly with his coaching career.



"He's not one of these who, as soon as they've retired, has jumped into a job. He's almost earned his stripes with coaching roles.

"He and Lee Carsley work quite well together as well."

Bent then proposed a scenario where if he was presented with the Cesena role or taking over Championship outfit Hull City, who sit fifth in the second tier, the former Premier League marksman wouldn't look too far away from home.



Hull or Cesena?

"I'd rather take the Hull job," Bent said.

"There's a chance to get into the Premier League next year. If a play-off team in the Championship can get into the Premier League, I'm going there."

4 Could Cole have kicked off his managerial career closer to home? Credit: Getty



Bent added: "Northern Italy sounds beautiful.

"But if he was to go to Hull, he's in the Premier League if they get promoted next year.

"Even if they don't get promoted, he's still in a very, very good league."

Although Hull stand a chance of returning to the English top flight for the first time since 2017, Cole could still feel the rush of promotion at Cesena.

How are Cesena performing this season?

Cesena sit 8th in Serie B, which would be good enough to secure a spot in the league's preliminary round for the promotion play-offs.

In Serie B, the top two teams in the 20-team division are automatically promoted to Serie A, with third and fourth progressing straight to the promotion play-offs semi-finals.



4 Cole has worked across several clubs and even for England under former interim boss Lee Carsley Credit: Getty

It means teams that finish 5th, 6th, 7th and 8th will have to take the longest route possible should they want to rub shoulders with the likes of Juventus and Inter Milan next season.

Teams in the Championship will soon become familiar with Serie B's play-off system as the EFL confirmed they will expand the play-offs from four teams to six, beginning with the 2026/27 campaign.

Although a surge to the top four of Serie B may be too difficult amid Cesena's surprising record, European football expert Andy Brassell believes the expectation for Cole will be to reach the play-offs.

Why Cole has a chance to 'make it his own'

"If you look at Cesena and where they are at the moment, they have a losing record," Brassell told talkSPORT.com.



"They've lost more games than they've won. So he's not expected to get into the top four. I think that would require an absolutely crazy run and a couple of teams above falling to bits.

"But it does mean that he needs to not just assure they make the top eight, but prep them for going into those playoffs.

4 Cole has been an assistant for five different teams but is finally making the step up Credit: Getty

"So it's quite a big job where he'll get a lot of time to work on the team and make it his own between now and the end of the season."

Despite the difficulty of the task that awaits, Brassell reserved special praise for Cole and his willingness to take the path less travelled to kick-start his managerial career.



"Look, Ashley Cole has proved so far by doing a variety of different jobs that he's a thoughtful coach," Brassell said.

"He's someone who's not turned his nose up at stuff that other big players would consider a bit menial. He's been really keen to learn the whole way.

"I think if you look at some people he knows, and I think you look at Cesc Fabregas and Dennis Wise, for example, there are people involved in Italian football who will speak for him, who know him. And I think that will have played a big part in him gettng involved there.

"But nevertheless, seeing a young English coach going there and having a go and trying to carve something out is really interesting."

Cole's first match in charge of Cesena is a trip to 15th-placed Mantova on Tuesday, with kick-off scheduled for 7pm.

Football.title: Liverpool vs Galatasaray LIVE commentary: Kick-off time, team news and how to follow Anfield Champions League clash
Football.content:
Liverpool vs Galatasaray LIVE commentary: Kick-off time, team news and how to follow Anfield Champions League clash

Liverpool face a crucial Champions League last 16 second leg at home to Galatasaray this week.

The Reds welcome the Turkish giants to Anfield as they look to overturn a narrow first leg deficit.

3 Liverpool must overturn a first leg deficit if they are to progress past Galatasaray Credit: Getty

Galatasaray claimed a 1-0 victory in Istanbul last week thanks to Mario Lemina's goal.



Both sides later had goals ruled out as the Premier League champions were beaten away from home.

Liverpool have not reached the quarter-finals of the Champions League since they were beaten by Real Madrid in the 2022 final.

Their last two appearances in the competition both ended at the last 16 stage, including last season's penalty shootout defeat to Paris Saint-Germain.

Meanwhile, Galatasaray last made the last eight 12 years ago in 2013.

A win at Anfield would be only their second competitive away victory over English opposition.



As for their previous away matches against Liverpool, they drew 0-0 in February 2002 before suffering a 3-2 defeat in September 2006.

The winner of the tie will face either PSG or Chelsea in next month's quarter-finals.



Liverpool vs Galatasaray: Date and how to follow

Liverpool welcome Galatasaray to Merseyside on Wednesday, March 18.

Kick-off in their second leg meeting is scheduled for 8pm and is live on talkSPORT.

3 Lemina's strike was the difference in last week's Champions League last 16 first leg Credit: Shutterstock Editorial



Adrian Durham will present live coverage from Anfield on Kick Off from 7.45pm.

The show follows the second leg between Barcelona and Newcastle United.

Tune in to talkSPORT through the website via the live stream or find us on DAB digital radio through your smart speaker and on 1089 or 1053 AM.

Download and listen to all our live commentaries via the talkSPORT app.

Visit talkSPORT BET for all the latest odds and bet boosts for every big event.



Subscribe to our talkSPORT YouTube channel for news, views and analysis plus our regular goals show and for more, 

3 Alisson is set to start in goal for Liverpool after returning to the side on Sunday Credit: Getty

Liverpool vs Galatasaray: Team news

Arne Slot was handed a boost over the weekend as Alisson returned in goal against Tottenham Hotspur.

The Brazilian had missed last Tuesday's first leg defeat through injury.

Federico Chiesa was also fit to feature off the bench against Spurs and could be involved on Wednesday.



Alexander Isak remains at least three weeks away from a long-awaited return to action.

Wataru Endo, Giovanni Leoni and Conor Bradley have all been ruled out for the season.

As for Galatasaray, they are set to be without defender Arda Unyay for their trip to England.

The teenager remains a fitness concern having also missed last week's first leg.

Unyay has been sidelined since January with a hamstring strain.

Football.title: Arsenal vs Bayer Leverkusen LIVE commentary: Kick-off time, team news and how to follow Champions League second leg
Football.content:
Arsenal vs Bayer Leverkusen LIVE commentary: Kick-off time, team news and how to follow Champions League second leg

Arsenal welcome Bayer Leverkusen to the Emirates tonight for the second leg of their Champions League last 16 tie.

The two sides played out a 1-1 draw in Germany last week to leave the contest in the balance.

3 Arsenal continue their push for a quadruple against Leverkusen this week Credit: Shutterstock Editorial

Leverkusen had led through Robert Andrich before Kai Havertz's late penalty for the visitors.



The winner of their meeting will face either Bodo/Glimt or Sporting Lisbon in the quarter-finals next month.

Arsenal are aiming to book a third consecutive appearance in the last eight.

Mikel Arteta's side have reached that stage every season since returning to the Champions League in 2023.

Prior to their current run, they had not made the quarter-finals since 2010, when they also did so three years in a row.



Meanwhile, Leverkusen could reach the last eight for the first time since making it all the way to the final in 2002.

However, they have won just two of their previous 13 away matches against English clubs, including November's win at Manchester City.



Their only previous trip to face Arsenal ended in a 4-1 defeat 24 years ago.

Arsenal vs Leverkusen: Date and how to follow

Arsenal host Leverkusen tonight - Tuesday, March 17 - with kick-off from the Emirates at 8pm.



talkSPORT will have live commentary from north London as the Gunners face their German opponents once again.

3 Leverkusen were pegged back late on in the first leg at home to Arsenal Credit: Getty

Adrian Durham presents coverage on the Kick Off from 7pm.

Tune in to talkSPORT through the website via the live stream or find us on DAB digital radio through your smart speaker and on 1089 or 1053 AM.

Download and listen to all our live commentaries via the talkSPORT app.

Visit talkSPORT BET for all the latest odds and bet boosts for every big event.



Subscribe to our talkSPORT YouTube channel for news, views and analysis plus our regular goals show and for more, 

Arsenal vs Leverkusen: Team news

Arteta has confirmed both Martin Odegaard and Jurrien Timber will miss the second leg.

The pair are also doubts for Sunday's Carabao Cup final against City at Wembley Stadium.

Mikel Merino also remains unavailable after being ruled out for the remainder of the season.

3 Arteta will be without Odegaard and Timber on Tuesday night Credit: Getty



Leandro Trossard is available, though, whilst Max Dowman could also feature.

The 16-year-old became the youngest scorer in Premier League history with his goal against Everton on Saturday.

As for the visitors, Leverkusen remain without Mark Flekken, Loic Bade, Arthur and Lucas Vazquez.

The quartet all missed last week's first leg for Kasper Hjulmand's side.

Arsenal vs Leverkusen: What's been said?

Speaking to reporters on Monday, Arteta discussed his expectations for the second leg.



The Arsenal boss explained: "Watching them play against Bayern Munich at the weekend, very similar.

"Really well coached, clear structure. We know what we have to do in front of our crowd. Hopefully we are going to do it."

Football.title: EFL club forced to postpone two games as statements released
Football.content:
Grimsby Town have been forced to postpone two fixtures due to international call-ups.

David Artell's side were scheduled to face Cambridge United on March 24 and Chesterfield on March 28 in League Two action.

3 Grimsby Town will have to make up two fixtures after the international break Credit: getty

However, those games have been pushed back as a result of the EFL's regulations regarding international call-ups.

According to the EFL's rules, Championship, League One and League Two fixtures scheduled during the international window can be postponed 'if a club receives a sufficient number of international call-ups'.

What is the EFL rule that allows clubs to request fixture postponements?

EFL clubs are allowed to request a postponement if 'at least three players are selected for their country's senior, Under-21, Under-20 or Under-19 teams.'

Grimsby fall into that category as Geza David Turi, Clarke Oduor and Jude Soonsup-Bell have all been called up for international action in March.

Turi will represent the Faroe Islands, Oduor will play for Kenya and Soonsup-Bell is set to feature for Thailand.

When will Grimsby play next?

Luckily for the Mariners, they will have the trio available for two more fixtures before they head off to their respective international camps.

Grimsby will enjoy a double header of home fixtures, with the first being against Fleetwood Town on Tuesday at 7:45pm.

Artell's side then face Barrow, who recently axed Dino Maamria after just six games, on Saturday at 3pm.

After the Barrow fixture, Grimsby won't be in action again until April 3, when they host Harrogate Town.

How have Grimsby performed this season?

The Mariners will aim to take maximum points from the two clashes before the international window as they sit 8th in League Two with 56 points, one place and three points outside of the play-off spots.

3 The Mariners will need to hit the ground running once the international break is over Credit: GETTY

In the fourth tier, the top three teams are automatically promoted to League One while those who finish from 4th to 7th qualify for the play-offs.

Grimsby will look to bounce back on Tuesday against Fleetwood having slipped to a 1-0 loss against Oldham Athletic on Saturday, with Artell believing his side did enough to avoid defeat.

"Of course, we want to respond straight away," Artell said.

"We didn't do ourselves justice and came out on the wrong side of a tight game.

"Having now watched it back, I haven't really changed my opinion - I thought it was a poor game.

"We were probably the better team second half, but not by much.

3 Grimsby are on track for their best finish in League Two since being promoted, but it could be for nought Credit: Getty

"We didn't do enough to win it, but probably not enough to lose it either. Those are the fine margins, so we lick our wounds and go again."

Despite the frustration of a second loss in their last 15 fixtures, Artell was keen to look forward as they continue their charge towards the play-offs.

"It's the old cliche - on to the next one," Artell said.

"This game is worth exactly the same as one in August or November.

"We've got to be us, be really good at what we do, and win the game. That's always the challenge."

Football.title: Joao Pedro panicked he’d been dropped from Brazil squad after Carlo Ancelotti blunder
Football.content:
Joao Pedro was left fearing the worst after Carlo Ancelotti initially appeared to leave him out of his Brazil squad.

The Chelsea star is set to join up with his national team for their upcoming friendlies against France and Croatia, but this was almost in doubt after a huge blunder from his manager.

4 After an initial mix-up, Joao Pedro now looks set to add to his six previous Brazil caps Credit: Getty

Awkward moment

On Monday, national team boss Ancelotti named his 26-man squad for their March internationals live on Brazilian channel Panela SporTV.



But there was a hairy moment for Pedro, as the Italian only announced the first 25 players to be called up.

The Blues forward is currently on a rich vein of form, with 11 goals in his last 14 games, meaning his absence would have shocked many.

Things soon became even more awkward, as Pedro's photo and name appeared on the screen in the background of the broadcast, despite his name not being called out.

Several minutes passed after Ancelotti's initial announcement before he was informed by national team director Rodrigo Caetano that he had accidentally left Pedro out of his squad.



The Italian quickly apologised for the major gaffe, and clarified that he had unintentionally skipped the 24-year-old's name.

Holding his hand up, Ancelotti told the media: "Sorry, I missed out Joao Pedro of Chelsea."



'Damn! I've been left out'

With his place in the Brazil squad now confirmed, Pedro later revealed his reaction to the eventful squad announcement.

Asked for his emotions when his name was missed out in an interview with Panela SporTv, the attacker opened up: "It always feels like the first time [to be called up] those butterflies in the stomach.



"And then when he [Ancelotti] finished the list I said to myself: 'Damn! I've been left out!!'

4 Pedro smiled as he discussed the whirlwind of emotions he felt watching Ancelotti's eventful squad announcement Credit: Panela SporTV

"Then everyone started calling me, I didn't know what was happening... then I came back and thought: "Damn, I'm in, I'm in!" haha."

Smiling as he shared his relief, Pedro finished off: "But it's the same feeling of butterflies ever since my first call up.

"I'm very happy. I'm in a really special moment in my life at the moment as well, and now let's go all out to beat France and Croatia."



Pedro will certainly be relieved to have made it into Ancelotti's 26-man squad for the upcoming games.

This is the last chance for the legendary manager to look at players before naming his final World Cup squad on May 18.

Despite only being capped six times previously for his national team. Pedro will fancy his chances of making the final cut, after a fine debut season for Chelsea which has seen him score 18 goals altogether.

4 Pedro scored for Chelsea in a recent FA Cup win against Wrexham Credit: Getty

4 Neymar was again overlooked by Ancelotti, and has not featured for Brazil since October 2023 Credit: Getty



And the Blues forward wasn't the only Premier League player to be selected by Ancelotti.

Brentford striker Igor Thiago, and Bournemouth winger Rayan both received their maiden call-ups, while Liverpool goalkeeper Alisson and Newcastle's Bruno Guimaraes were again chosen.

So too were Arsenal defender Gabriel Magalhaes, Manchester United veteran Casemiro and Pedro's Chelsea teammate Andrey Santos.

Meanwhile, football megastar Neymar was again left out of the squad despite speculation he would make his long-awaited return to the national team fold.

Football.title: Gianluca Nani signed ‘special’ transfer after chance car ride to maintain 51-year Wrexham record
Football.content:
Gianluca Nani has confirmed he told Watford's latest star that 'one day we will go together' mere months before it became a reality.

The 63-year-old, who is the Group Technical Director for both Udinese and the Hornets, has signed some of European football's greatest talents across his career, from Roberto Baggio to Pep Guardiola.

4 Gianluca Nani confirmed a chance meeting at an airport led Watford to sign Edoardo Bove Credit: Getty

His latest coup was helping convince Edoardo Bove to join Championship Watford following his exit from Roma.



The Italy Under-21 international hadn't played a minute of football since suffering an on-pitch cardiac arrest in December 2024.

While on loan at Fiorentina, Bove collapsed during a Serie A game against Inter Milan and was later fitted with a cardiac defibrillator.

As a result, the 23-year-old midfielder was unable to continue his career in his homeland and was released from his contract at Roma.

Nani, who was more than familiar with his compatriot due to his work at Udinese, then had a chance encounter with Bove last May, when the latter was Fiorentina's guest of honour for their trip to Udine.



Nine months later, Nani joined talkSPORT.com the morning after I Viola returned to the Bluenergy Stadium, where fittingly the conversation turned to Bove.

"I met him by chance in Udine," the sporting and technical director revealed. "He was coming here to watch the last game of last season.



"That was Udinese-Fiorentina. He was following Fiorentina. And then he was without a car. I gave him a lift.

"During the lift, I started to talk. I said, 'One day we will go together.' But I couldn't expect it so quickly and so directly.



"But that is not me. It's always the club and all the staff of people that work in the club that do this kind of job."

4 Bove chose to restart his career at Watford over options across Europe

Wrexham's horror Watford record

Bove has already made five appearances for Edward Still's side, four of which have come in Watford's last four matches.

He is expected to be part of the squad, hoping to bounce back from their 3-1 loss to Stoke City on Saturday against Wrexham this week.

Wrexham have won only one of their previous 10 Football League trips to Watford (D2 L7); a 2-1 victory 51 years ago in March 1975.



Bove was billed as a 'top, proven talent' upon signing a five-and-a-half-year deal at Vicarage Road at the end of January.

And Nani proved how excited Watford fans can be when he personally namechecked him while assessing the star names who he'd had a hand in signing or developing throughout his illustrious career.

4 Watford's slim playoff hopes will effectively be ended with a defeat to Wrexham Credit: Getty

Bove 'one of many special players'

Nani added to talkSPORT: "I'm proud of all my players. I'm not the one that I love more than another.

"Then of course, when you talk about special players, you talk about Baggio and Guardiola. But there are many, many, many of them.



"It's the same when I talk about Udinese and Watford. It's a group. It's like [saying] you have two sons, which one do you like more?

"That's why Baggio and Guardiola are the most famous, they're unbelievable. But many of them, Marek Hamšík [at Brescia] was 15 years old. A special player, many, many of them.

"Now, also, we sign [for] Watford, Edoardo Bove, that is coming from a special situation. Here in Udine, we have [Nicolo] Zaniolo.

4 Nani credited the work of owner Giampaolo Pozzo (C) in building the philosophy behind signing young stars Credit: Getty

"That is a player with ability. He could play with a super top club.

But also a young player like the last two. Like [Juan David] Arizala, [Branimir] Mlačić, that would be top talent.



"In Watford, we have [Nestory] Irankunda, [Othmane] Maamma. So many, many, many players. It's difficult for me to say.

"I'm proud of my staff and people. First of all, because all together, that is a job that has to be done. Never will be one person who does this job.

"But it's always a staff of people. Always say that a good staff of people with the same mentality, same winning mentality, is much more than any great individual.

"So that's why, that is the way that we work in Udine. That is the way that we work also in the other club, in the group of Watford.

"Trying all together to go in the same direction, following the same philosophy. Philosophy that was built by Mr Pozzo 40 years ago. And that is the way that we keep doing, and we hope we will do it."

Football.title: talkSPORT tips – Best football bets and expert advice on Tuesday 17 March
Football.content:
English teams will be hoping for a marked turnaround in Champions League fortunes this week as the first of the second-leg round-of-16 fixtures take place on Tuesday.

While there is also a bumper programme of EFL fixtures with massive implications in both the play-off and relegation battles.

GET £40 IN FREE BETS WHEN YOU BET ON THE FOOTBALL WITH talkSPORT BET

Tuesday football tips

Bet of the Day - Paris Saint-Germain to beat Chelsea @ 2/1

Chelsea have work to do to turn their Champions League tie around after suffering a 5-2 defeat in Paris last week, admittedly with the scores having been level going into the 74th minute.

After that late flurry, Paris Saint-Germain already have one foot in the quarter-finals which perhaps negates the necessity for them to go for the win.

That might temper the enthusiasm for some for an away victory but it also plays into the prices and they simply look too big to ignore at 2/1, particularly with Chelsea having to push from the start.

The Blues have won four out of four at Stamford Bridge in Europe with a standout being the 3-0 beating of Barcelona, but that was back in November and recent signs are not so encouraging.

Liam Rosenior's men were beaten 1-0 by Newcastle last time out at home, were taken to extra time by Wrexham in the FA Cup, lost home and away to Arsenal in the League Cup, while struggling Burnley and Leeds have left the Bridge with a point too. West Ham might have done more than that but surrendered a 2-0 lead to lose 3-2.

PSG, who won 2-1 In Barcelona and thrashed Bayer Leverkusen 7-2 at the BayArena in the League Phase, are a different level to those sides, barring Arsenal perhaps, and have also had the weekend off, and their enviable host of attacking options can pick holes in the hosts' fragile rear-guard to make a comfortable passage through the last eight.

Bet Builder - Arsenal to win, Under 2.5 Goals, Gabriel Martinelli to score @ 11/1

Bayer Leverkusen performed admirably in the first leg and given it was a late and somewhat controversial penalty that Arsenal scored, they may feel aggrieved that they are not taking an advantage to the Emirates.

However, their chance of an upset looks to have gone and Arsenal can maintain their perfect home record in Europe, having already beaten Bayern Munich and Atletico Madrid at the Emirates.

Don't expect a goalfest though, particularly with the tie poised at 1-1 and Leverkusen likely to set up to be hard to beat.

Since suffering their only home defeat in the Premier League though, the Gunners have reeled off five successive wins at the Emirates, three of which came with a clean sheet.

Mikel Arteta's men will eventually find a way to win and one player who could help them do that is Gabriel Martinelli, who leads the way for Arsenal with six goals so far in the Champions League, two more than Viktor Gyokeres.

While not always a starter, the fact Martinelli was on the bench at the weekend suggests he could be given the nod on Tuesday, and having scored against the might of Bayern and Atletico already in Europe, he boosts the Bet Builder to a juicy 11/1.

Acca: Wrexham to beat Watford, Bradford City to beat Mansfield, Cambridge United to beat Walsall @ 21/2

Wrexham are a team in form and look generously priced to take care of an erratic Watford side whose play-off challenge has faltered at the wrong time.

There was an air of understanding in the Red Dragons' defeat against Hull, perhaps still suffering with a hangover from taking Chelsea to extra time in the FA Cup, but they bounced back with a 2-0 win over Swansea on Friday night.

The Hornets went down 3-1 at Stoke a day later and have now won only two of their last 12 games in the Championship, losing five, while failing to beat the likes of Sheffield Wednesday and a free-falling Preston.

Wrexham were held to a 2-2 draw in the reverse fixture but are in a stronger position now and can go one better as they look to stay ahead of the chasing pack.

Bradford City have been a little inconsistent of late but can consolidate their play-off position with another three points on home territory. The Bantams have won 14 of their 18 league games at the University of Bradford Stadium, losing only twice, and they can chalk up another against FA Cup heroes Mansfield.

The Stage have been beaten by struggling Blackpool and held by third-bottom Rotherham on their last away league games, last winning on the road in the third tier in December.

Cambridge United are three points behind MK Dons in second in League Two so will be desperate to make use of their game in hand when they take on Walsall.

Saturday's 5-0 win over Gillingham should have restored confidence after successive draws, although they are unbeaten in seven. The Saddlers have lost more at home than they have won, and have not picked up three points at the Bescot since Boxing Day, losing five of their seven games since, including each of the last four.

News.title: Singer, 27, killed in double-decker bus crash days before clinching deal to make her a music mega-star
News.content:
CRASH TRAGEDY Singer, 27, killed in double-decker bus crash days before clinching deal to make her a music mega-star

AN aspiring singer died in a tragic crash with a double-decker bus just days before a life-changing meeting for her career.

Soriah Barry died in a devastating early-morning collision in east London after dropping off her friend.





The 27-year-old was rushed to hospital just before 7am on February 8, 2024, but tragically died from her injuries.

Her mum Saphiatu has revealed Soriah was about to meet with Apple Music the week after she died, in the hope they would release her R&B songs.

Soriah was a promising young singer, following in the footsteps of her musical powerhouse aunt Jamilah Barry.

Her mum told the Metro she’s kept the 27-year-old’s room exactly the same since she died – except for throwing away KFC boxes.



Aunt Malika Barry added: “Everything was working well for her.

“The music was going so well, she had a meeting with Apple Music and was going on Spotify. She was so talented and beautiful – she was the full package.”

Apple Music told Soriah’s family they want to upload her songs in memory of the young woman.

She had just returned from a weekend away with friends in an AirBnB when she crashed.



CCTV showed Soriah’s car veering towards a stopped bus in Hackney before a “bang like a firework” was heard and smoke erupted from both vehicles.

An inquest heard she was travelling at around 35mph, after accelerating from 23mph seconds before.

The bus driver said that thought Soriah had crossed the road, intending to park in front of the bus.

The inquest heard from a witness who rushed to help the young singer.

He recalled how Soriah was covered by airbags and he asked if she was okay, and she replied: “No, I’m not.”



Members of the public rushed to help as she went into cardiac arrest, before emergency services arrived.

It took two hours for her to get to hospital, where her mum was waiting, in rush hour traffic.

Horrifically, some witnesses filmed her lying on the road with her clothes open and uploaded the footage to TikTok.



Her cause of death was given as internal bleeding and severe damage to her liver.

The inquest heard she had been to a party the night before but was a “safe driver” and she was below the drink and drive limit.

The coroner concluded she hadn’t been wearing a seatbelt.

But the inquest couldn’t determine a reason why the car crashed.

Her family believe she was distracted at the wheel of the car which they say often veered to the side, and they think she accidentally accelerated instead of braking.



The coroner said: “For approximately three seconds Soriah failed to take evasive action. I cannot say what caused Soriah’s lack of awareness.”

A statement from the family said: “Soriah was a loyal person always there for everyone in life. She was an aspiring singer.

“She was an inspiring person who would have a massive impression on you. She was very very loved. We will never get over this.

“She was just an amazing person who will always be in our hearts.”

RECOMMENDED STORIES

A GoFundMe by her aunt Jamilah read: “Soriah was fierce, loyal and wow – so full of life. She touched so many hearts, and we want to honour her with the farewell she deserves.



“Losing her so suddenly has been devastating, and any contribution – no matter how small – would mean the world to us.”

News.title: Grief author who wrote book about husband’s death is found guilty of murdering him to erase debt and run off with lover
News.content:
A CHILDREN’S author has been found guilty of poisoning her husband after writing a book about grief following his death.

Kouri Richins, 35, fatally poisoned Eric – her husband and father to her three children – when she spiked his Moscow Mule cocktail with a deadly dose of fentanyl.





The court heard she slipped at least five times the lethal dose of the synthetic opioid into her hubby’s drink in March 2022.

She believed she would inherit more than $4 million if he were to die and run away with her handyman lover, the court was told.

The jury in Park City, Utah, handed down the guilty verdict after deliberating for just three hours following a three-week trial.

Mother-of-three, Richins audaciously penned a book about her own children’s grief after the murder of their father.

READ MORE WORLD NEWS VILE VID Sick moment schoolgirls laugh in cop car after 'plot to kill child & drink blood'

A month before her arrest in May 2023, Richins appeared in a local news interview discussing her book, Are You With Me?

During the news segment, Richins explained that she wrote the book to help children who experienced the sudden loss of a parent or loved one.

On Monday, she was found guilty of aggravated murder, attempted aggravated murder, insurance fraud, and forgery charges.



She was also found guilty of attempting to poison him a month prior on Valentine’s Day with a fentanyl-laced sandwich.

Prosecutor Brad Bloodworth told jurors the author’s home-flipping business had been $4.5 million in debt when she hatched the sinister plot to collect Eric’s estate and run off to start a new life with her lover, Robert Josh Grossman.

Bloodworth said Richins was an “incompetent” business owner who “carefully curated the facade of a privileged, affluent, successful business owner”.

She lived with her husband and children in Kamas, a suburb in the affluent ski town of Park City in Utah.

He said the only “way forward” she could see, was for “Eric to die”.



The court heard that Richins asked Grossman, her 43-year-old Iraq War veteran lover, if he’d ever killed anyone just days after she slipped Eric the fatal dose of fentanyl.

The handyman testified in the trial, saying she had first asked the question in March 2022 when the pair discussed Eric’s death for the first time.

Prosecutors showed the jury text messages between Richins and Grossman.

The messages show her fantasizing about leaving her husband, gaining millions in a divorce, and marrying her secret lover.

“I’m in love with a man that’s not my husband,” Richins wrote.



“I want to but can’t break up my family.

“It’s having your cake and eating it too. I do just want to love you. I do love you.”



Internet history from her phone also showed searches including “what is a lethal dose of fentanyl,” “luxury prisons for the rich America,” and “if someone is poisoned what does it go down on the death certificate as,” according to a digital forensic analyst.

“She wanted to leave Eric Richins but did not want to leave his money,” Bloodworth told the court.

“Their prenup meant if she left him, she would also leave most of his money.”

The court heard she had opened numerous life insurance policies on Eric without his knowledge, according to prosecutors.

The 911 call was also replayed for the jury from the night of her husband’s death.



Defense attorney Wendy Lewis argued that the prosecution “looks at facts one way and sees a witch, but if you look at those facts another way, you see a widow.”

Making a bid for a mistrial, the failed house flipper’s other defence lawyer Kathy Nester claimed Bloodworth had mistepped throughout his closing.

Her motion was smacked down by Judge Richard Mrazik.

Richins, who pleaded not guilty to the crimes, could now face up to 25 years in prison.

She will return to court for sentencing on May 13—the day her husband would have turned 44.



After the verdict, family members on both sides of the couple’s family left the trial hugging and crying.

Eric Richins’ sister, Amy Richins, spoke to media outside the courthouse.

“Honestly I feel like we’re all in shock. It’s been a long time coming,” she said.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

She added that the family can now focus on honouring her brother and supporting his sons.

“Just very happy that we got justice for my brother.”

News.title: Household Cavalry trooper who got fellow soldier drunk then sexually assaulted his sleeping girlfriend faces deportation
News.content:
SICK ATTACK Household Cavalry trooper who got fellow soldier drunk then sexually assaulted his sleeping girlfriend faces deportation

A HOUSEHOLD Cavalry soldier is facing deportation after getting one of his colleagues blackout drunk and sexually assaulting his girlfriend.

Britain’s top military judge warned Joeli Ratu, 29, that he was facing being sent back to Fiji after he sentenced to the soldier to 11 months in military detention over the frightening sex attack.





A court heard Ratu made a “concoction” for a fellow trooper that made him pass out before assaulting the drunk man’s sleeping girlfriend.

Ratu waited until his comrade was unconscious before entering the soldier’s bedroom at Hyde Park Barracks in Knightsbridge and subjecting the sleeping woman to a terrifying 20-minute assault.

The victim told a military court that she “felt like a mouse trapped in the corner” as Ratu grabbed her and tried to convince her to have sex with him.

Ratu denied two counts of sexual assault but was found guilty after a three day trial at Bulford Military Court.

The Household Cavalry trooper met the woman early one evening when they were out drinking at a bar with other soldiers.

On the night of the assault Ratu had taken an “immediate interest” in his fellow soldier’s girlfriend, the court was told.

After a few drinks, the group all went back to the boyfriend’s flat at Hyde Park Barracks and continued drinking.

The woman was “exhausted” and after three drinks she went to bed in the flat.



Ratu stayed longer than everyone else and proceeded to make a strong alcoholic “concoction” for his fellow soldier that caused him to pass out.

The boyfriend took himself to the bathroom after becoming too drunk and asked Ratu to let himself out of the flat.

Instead of leaving, however, Ratu went into his colleague’s room, where he knew the man’s girlfriend was asleep.

She slept shirtless with a pair of shorts, and told the court she felt safe even though the lock on the door was broken because she thought a barracks would be the “safest place.”

But she woke up to find Ratu on top of her.



The victim told the court: “When I woke up Ratu was over the top of me. I remember waking up and seeing a face next to my face.

“It was split second where I saw it and jumped back. I was like ‘what’s going on, where’s my boyfriend?’

“I can’t get up and run because he’s over me and in front of the exit. He’s trying to convince me to sleep with him.



“I kept backing off but every time I went further away he got closer to me, no matter how far away I moved. He tried to grab me, and I was very adamant about asking where my boyfriend was.

“I kept thinking Jesus Christ, what have I got into? If I don’t do something, something is going to go terribly wrong.

“It was like a cat trying to pounce on something. Like cat and mouse, and I felt like a mouse trapped in the corner.

“He’s frantic while I’m trying to remain composed. I remember him saying I should be his one night stand and that my boyfriend was passed out.

“He kept saying there’s no one here and your boyfriend’s gone. Trying to tell me that there’s no option, this is the only option. He won’t stop advancing on me as if it’s going to change my mind.



“I don’t know what was going through his mind to go into a room where a girl is sleeping and get on top of her.

“I can’t understand it. He kept asking for sex and trying to kiss me.”

After 20 horrifying minutes Ratu listened to the woman’s pleas and left to fetch her boyfriend.

“He came in carrying my boyfriend and dumped him on the bed”, she said.

“He was so, so sick. Ratu said ‘I’m sorry for getting him so drunk’ – and that wording stuck out to me because my boyfriend was capable of making his own decisions.”



Ratu eventually left the flat but tried to kiss the woman’s neck again as he went, she reported the terrifying incident the following day.

Defending the trooper, Ruba Huleihel said that he was born to two pastors in Fiji and moved here in 2019 to fulfil a “childhood dream” of serving in the British Army.

She said: “Because he is a foreign national a custodial sentence could identify him as a candidate for deportation by the Home Office.

“That would have a devastating impact on him and his family. He hopes to apply for citizenship soon.

“It will mean the loss of his career that was a childhood dream for him.”



Ratu has been sentenced to 11 months’ service detention, placed on the sex offenders register for five years and been kicked from the armed forces.

Judge Advocate General Alan Large apologised to the former soldier “if he is deported” because of the sentence.

The judge concluded: “Sexual offending undermines the bond of trust that exists between those who serve together. It affects morale and overall effects operational effectiveness.

“The effect on morale and the adverse effect on the reputation of the armed forces. This is just the sort of offending that young female service personnel fear.

“You entered her bedroom while she was asleep, half-naked and vulnerable. You persisted in your assault of her and caused her a lot of harm.



RECOMMENDED STORIES

“We consider it in the interest of justice to send you to the military corrective training centre in Colchester. It will reduce the burden on the prison system and let you use the excellent facilities there.

“If that means you will be deported then we are sorry.”

News.title: Brit businessman, 33, found dead in a canal after CCTV showed him ‘pacing relentlessly’ in front of hotel in Thailand
News.content:
DEATH RIDDLE Brit businessman, 33, found dead in a canal after CCTV showed him ‘pacing relentlessly’ in front of hotel in Thailand

A BRIT businessman has been found dead in a canal after he was seen pacing erratically in Thailand.

The 33-year-old investment adviser was reportedly captured on CCTV pacing restlessly outside a hotel.





Then his body was discovered floating in a drainage ditch on popular holiday island Phuket, near the idyllic Bang Tao Beach.

Cops rushed to the scene after the corpse was found by a shocked Burmese worker on 14 March.

The man was floating face-down, barefoot and wearing a grey shirt and black pants, police said.

They said that the Brit had begun to bloat from being in the water so long.

Cops found no signs of an assault, with his key card for a nearby hotel in his pocket.

His new iPhone 17 had been left on the bank nearby.

Local Police Chief Sutthirak Chuthong confirmed that the victim had been identified as a British national.



He added that the man’s hotel had been unable to contact him, saying: “A search of the room revealed no signs of forced entry or other irregularities.”

Cops think the body had been in the canal for no more than two days.

Lieutenant Colonel Thongchai Matitham added: “At around 12.30pm on Saturday, March 14, a worker from Myanmar harvesting morning glory vegetables came across the body and alerted the police station.

“When officers arrived at the scene, the body was found lying face down in a public drainage canal.

“An iPhone 17 was discovered on the bank nearby and had not fallen into the water.



“No other valuables were found, and the deceased was not wearing shoes.”

Hotel records showed the man he had checked in alone.

He had been due to leave the hotel the day before the body was discovered, but never checked out.

Matitham added: “CCTV footage showed that he appeared restless and was moving back and forth in front of the hotel before leaving the area.

“His movements eventually led to the location where his body was later discovered.”



Cops are awaiting autopsy results “to determine whether drugs were present in his system”.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

A full investigation has been launched.

The Sun contacted the UK Foreign Office for comment.

News.title: Banksy’s ‘real name finally revealed in old police report’ after famed graffiti artist’s ‘misdemeanour’ in New York
News.content:
BANKSY UNMASKED Banksy’s ‘real name finally revealed in old police report’ after famed graffiti artist’s ‘misdemeanour’ in New York

BANKSY’S identity has reportedly finally been uncovered in a 26-year-old police report.

The real name of the world’s most famous graffiti artist is said to be Robin Gunningham after records of a misdemeanour in New York in 2000 were found.





It has long been speculated that this was Banksy’s real name but news agency Reuters believe they can finally confirm his identity after an investigation.

It also claimed that Gunningham changed his legal name to David Jones – one of the UK’s most common – to avoid detection.

Under this new name he travelled to Ukraine where Banksy painted murals. He left in October 2022 on the same day as Robert del Naja a member of Massive Attack thought to be in the artist’s circle.

It was even once suggested that Robert was the artist himself as he is also a graffiti artist.

Reuters also found that the date on Jones’s passport matched the one on Gunningham’s documents.

Banksy emerged out of the Bristol art scene in the late 1990s and his identity has remained a mystery throughout his thirty year career.



It was previously reported that Robin Gunningham, a student at Bristol Cathedral School, adopted the name Robin Banks before becoming Banksy.

Even those who have parted ways with the famous graffiti artist, such as his former manager Steve Lazarides, have kept his name a secret.

Reuters said there was a public interest in knowing the identity of any public figure who has an influence on discourse.

The street artist’s stencil paintings are known for their sharp social commentary and have become incredibly valuable.

Banksy has said nothing following the investigation but his lawyer Mark Stephens said he didn’t accept “many of the details contained within your inquiry are correct”.



The representative added that the report could put the artist in danger and would interfere with his work.

Banksy was first named as Robin Gunningham in 2008 in a newspaper article but his then-manager denied it.

Gunningham was arrested in September 2000 after a Manhattan billboard for Marc Jacobs was defaced and he confessed, Reuters reports.

The “artist” had given the model “goofy teeth” and drawn a “giant speech bubble” but he didn’t get the chance to fill it before police arrested him.

Police documents, a handwritten confession and court filings from the time identify Gunningham as the vandal, the report found.



RECOMMENDED STORIES

The confession reads: ““The evening the night of September 17th I had been out drinking at a nightclub with friends when I decide to make a humorous adjustment to a billboard on top of the property on Hudson st.”

Gunningham posted bail for $1,500 and the felony offences were reduced to a misdemeanour charge. After a $310 fine and five days of community service he was good to go.

News.title: Fury as hundreds of mafia prisoners including murderers and terrorists to be dumped on holiday island loved by Brits
News.content:
CRIMINAL COLONY Fury as hundreds of mafia prisoners including murderers and terrorists to be dumped on holiday island loved by Brits

MOBSTERS and convicted terrorists will soon be dumped on one of Italy’s paradise islands on the Mediterranean Sea.

Frequently visited by Brits, Sardinia is famous for its white sandy beaches and turquoise bays – a postcard-perfect image that could be tarnished by hundreds of maximum-security prisoners.





Under the government of Giorgia Meloni, the island has been ordered to take in about 250 top-level mafia bosses, killers, and domestic terrorists.

This represents a third of the 750 high-risk inmates being consolidated into the handful of maximum-security facilities.

The plan will see them arrive in the next few weeks – despite protests by locals, who do not want the island to revert back to being seen as a hub for the “highest echelons” of Italy‘s mobsters.

“Sardinia does not deserve to be seen as Italy‘s Cayenne,” said governor Alessandra Todde, invoking the notorious former French Guiana penal colony on Devil’s Island.



“We have always done our bit in the fight against the mafia and organised crime, but we cannot accept such an unfair distribution [of prisoners].

“These are men who represent the highest echelons of mafia organisations, from the ’Ndrangheta to the Camorra, and they will have a profound impact on the island.”

In February, a rally saw more than 1,500 people – regional administrators, mayors, trade unions, associations and citizens – gather in Sardinia against the plan.

Todde told La Repubblica that she had been appealing for months to stop the transfer, but to no avail.



“We have written to Giorgia Meloni, but we’ve had no response,” she said. We are not asking for special privileges – just respect.”

The inmates will be split up between three jails – in the capital, Cagliari, the central city of Nuoro and Sassari in the north – and kept in strict near-total isolation under Italy’s controversial 41bis regime.

Named after an article of the Italian criminal code, it is among the most restrictive in Europe.

Introduced in their current form in 1992, after the mafia killings of judges Giovanni Falcone and Paolo Borsellino, the measures were originally meant to cut off mafia bosses from the outside world and other inmates, and to stop them from ordering criminal acts from behind bars.

While the restrictions are usually reserved for mobsters, they can now be imparted on people who have been found guilty of crimes ranging from terrorism to tobacco smuggling and kidnapping.



There is also the fear that the government’s plan could push mafia clans to relocate from mainland Italy to be near their jailed relatives.

This would be creating opportunities to launder illicit money and infiltrate business, particularly in less developed areas.

Maria Cristina Ornano, head of the sentence enforcement tribunal in Cagliari, said police and the judiciary would need increased security resources if more mobsters arrive.

“Once organised crime takes root here, we will not be able to get rid of it,” she said last month.

“We can see it in parts of southern Italy, which are among the most economically and socially deprived areas.”



Tourism accounts for just 7 per cent of output in the Nuoro region, 2025 Chamber of Commerce data show, where the economy mainly relies on agriculture and is dominated by small firms.

Pietro Borrotzu, a Catholic priest who runs a rehabilitation cooperative for prisoners in Nuoro, said dangerous working conditions and low salaries provide an ideal environment for the clans.

He said: “In this kind of context, organised crime could find plenty of foot soldiers.”

Business lobby Confindustria described what will be happening to Nuoro as “punishment.”

RECOMMENDED STORIES

Pierpaolo Milia, the group’s local head, said: “We are more of an island than Sardinia itself, far from ports and airports.



“Business incentives would be needed, and instead we are being punished with 41bis inmates.”

News.title: Is Britain hooked on government handouts we can’t afford? VOTE NOW
News.content:
POLL OF THE DAY Is Britain hooked on government handouts we can’t afford? VOTE NOW

SIR Keir Starmer has announced an emergency £53m support package for “vulnerable” households hit by the spike in the price of heating oil.

The PM said yesterday that the funds will go to “those households that are most exposed” to eye-watering price hikes.



As turmoil in the Middle East drives up global oil prices, Brits face increasing pressure on household budgets.

Funding has been allocated based on census data, “reflecting where the greatest need is.”



If the war on Iran continues to rage on, the PM could face even louder calls for a package of Government cash for households struggling with their gas and electricity bills.

With Britain already drowning in more than £2.8trillion of debt, questions remain about whether the UK can afford further handouts.

So what do YOU think, is Britain hooked on government handouts we can’t afford? Have your say in our exclusive poll.

Love to debate? Join Sun Club for just £1.99 a month for the boldest opinion on the issues shaping Britain.

News.title: Shocking moment drunk woman falls out of moving taxi onto busy road & ends up under wheel after fighting with friend
News.content:
THIS is the shocking moment a drunk woman falls out of a moving cab onto a busy road before getting run over by the taxi.

Horrifying footage shows the rear passenger door being pushed open as the taxi drives along a four-lane road.





A pair of kicking legs emerge and seconds later the driver turns on the car’s hazard lights while steering the vehicle towards the side of the road.

The woman appears to struggle before she tumbles out of the car and lands on the tarmac.

Her arm disappears for a moment under one of the taxi’s wheels as she lies in the middle of the busy road.



Dashcam footage then shows the car come to a stop as someone steps out.

Police said the incident happened in Xi’an, in central China on March 12.

Cops received a report after the footage went viral.

They said the woman, known by the surname Cheng, had been travelling in the taxi with a female friend after the pair had been drinking.



The two women began arguing during the journey, police said.

The woman who fell suffered minor abrasions, according to a police statement issued on 13th March.

Officials said the pair later continued their journey in the same taxi after the incident.

Police added that the case had been ruled out as a criminal matter.



But Cheng was slapped with a penalty for jumping from a moving vehicle by traffic cops.

It comes after footage of a toddler tumbling out of a moving car on a busy road in California went viral earlier this year.

Terrifying dashcam video showed the 19-month-old baby flying out of a moving car.

The passenger door flapped open as the car came to a sudden stop, almost causing a crash with a car behind it.

The baby was then seen sitting on the road in the middle of a busy junction, defenceless.



A car stopped just inches away from the small child before the tot’s mum leapt out of her vehicle and grabbed the baby.
